                                            <mjx-a= ssistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                    xmlns=3D"http://w=ww.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                                    <mi>K</mi>
                                                </math></mjx-assis= tive-mml>
                                        </mjx-container>
                                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">is moderately connected=E2=80=93e.g., if =
                                        </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" j=ax=3D"CHTML"
                                            tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"54" style=3D"font-size: 117=.4%;
                                            position: relative;">
                                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">=
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D43E TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mi>=
                                            </mjx-math>
                                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">
                                                <math=xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                                    <mi>K</mi>
                                                    <= /math>
                                            </mjx-assistive-mml>
                                        </mjx-container>
                                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">has at least one positive row/column pair=
                                            =E2=80=93then Osborne=E2=80=99s algorithm initially converges
                                            exponentially
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">fast, yielding an improved runtime=
                                        </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0"=jax=3D"CHTML"
                                            tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"55" style=3D"font-size: 11=7.4%;
                                            position: relative;">
                                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true"=>
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mi=>
                                                    <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                                            <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                <mjx-c clas=s=3D"mjx-c1D45A TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                </= mjx-mi>
                                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mj=x-c2212"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mn>
                                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-n qopname" space=3D"2">
                                                                    <= mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6C">
                                                                        </mjx-c>
                                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6F"></mjx-c>
                                                                        <mjx-c cla=ss=3D"mjx-c67"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                                                    <mjx-c=class=3D"mjx-c2061"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c1D705 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n Ma=thClass-close">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                                    </mjx-m= ath>
                                                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                            xmlns==3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"
                                                            display=3D"inline">
                                                            <mi>O</mi>
                                                            <mrow>=
                                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                                <mrow>
                                                                    <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">m=F0=9D=
                                                                        =9C=96</mi>
                                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                                                    <mn>1</mn>
                                                                    <mi class=3D="qopname">log</mi>
                                                                    <mo>=E2=81=A1</mo>
                                                                    <mi>=CE=BA</mi>
                                                                </mrow>
                                                                <mo class=3D"Math=Class-close">)</mo>
                                                            </mrow>
                                                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                        </mjx-container><span=class=3D"cmr-9">.
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">We also address numerical precisio=
                                            n issues by showing that these runtime bounds
                                            still hold when using</span>
                                        <!-- l. 30 -->
                                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                                            tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"56" style=3D"font-size=: 117.4%;
                                            position: relative;">
                                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"t=rue">
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mj= x-mi>
                                                    <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                                            <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-n qopname">=
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6C"></mjx-c>
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6F"></mjx-c>
                                                                <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c67"></mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mi>
                                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                                                <mjx-c= class=3D"mjx-c2061">
                                                                    </mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mo>
                                                            <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                                                <mjx-mo class==3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                                <mjx-mr= ow>
                                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                        <mjx-c= class=3D"mjx-c1D705 TEX-I">
                                                                            </mjx-c>
                                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathCla=ss-bin" space=3D"3">
                                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2F"></mjx-c>
                                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                                    <mjx-mi class==3D"mjx-i" space=3D"3">
                                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                                                    <= /mjx-mrow>
                                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29">=
                                                                            </mjx-c>
                                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-clo=se">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                            </mjx-math>
                                            <mjx-as= sistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                    xmlns=3D"http://ww=w.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                                    <mi>O</mi>
                                                    <mrow>
                                                        <mo class=3D"=MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                        <mrow>
                                                            <mi class=3D"qopname">log</mi>
                                                            <mo>=E2=81=A1</mo=>
                                                                <mrow>
                                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                                    <mrow>
                                                                        <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">=
                                                                            n=CE=BA</mi>
                                                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=95</mo>
                                                                        <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>=
                                                                    </mrow>
                                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                                                </mrow>
                                                        </mrow>
                                                        <mo class=3D"MathC=lass-close">)</mo>
                                                    </mrow>
                                                </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                        </mjx-container><span c=lass=3D"cmr-9">-bit
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">numbers. Our results are establish=
                                            ed through a potential argument that leverages
                                            a convex optimization
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">perspective of Osborne=E2=80=99s a=
                                            lgorithm, and relates the per-iteration progress to the
                                            current imbalance
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">as measured in Hellinger distance.=
                                            Unlike previous analyses, we critically
                                            exploit log-convexity of the
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">potential. Our analysis extends to=
                                            other variants of Osborne=E2=80=99s algorithm: along
                                            the way, we establish
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">significantly improved runtime bou=
                                            nds for cyclic, greedy, and parallelized
                                            variants.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Alyahya2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-24" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Alyahya, Hana, Rashid Mehmood, and Iyad Kati=
            b. =E2=80=9CParallel Iterative Solution of Large
            Sparse Linear Equation Systems on the Intel MIC Architecture=E2=
            =80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">Smart
                Infrastructure and
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">Applications: Foundations for Sm=
                arter Cities and Societies</span>. Ed. by
            Rashid Mehmood et al.
            Cham: Springer International Publishing, 2020, pp. 377=E2=80=93=
            407. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">isbn</span></span>=
            : 978-3-030-13705-2. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">do=
                    i</span></span>:
            10.1007/978-3-030-13705-2=CB=9916.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Many important scientific,
                engineering, and smart city applications require solving lar=
                ge
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">sparse linear equation systems. Th=
                    e numerical methods for solving linear
                    equations can be categorised
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">into direct methods and iterative =
                    methods. Jacobi method is one of the iterative
                    solvers that has been
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">widely used due to its simplicity =
                    and efficiency. Its performance is affected by
                    factors including the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">storage format, the specific compu=
                    tational algorithm, and its implementation.
                    While the performance
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of Jacobi has been studied extensi=
                    vely on conventional CPU architectures,
                    research on its performance
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">on emerging architectures, such as=
                    the Intel Many Integrated Core (MIC)
                    architecture, is still in its
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">infancy. In this chapter, we inves=
                    tigate the performance of parallel
                    implementations of the Jacobi
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">method on Knights Corner (KNC), th=
                    e first generation of the Intel MIC
                    architectures. We implement
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Jacobi with two storage formats, C=
                    ompressed Sparse Row (CSR) and Modified Sparse
                    Row (MSR), and
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">measure their performance in terms=
                    of execution time, offloading time, and
                    speedup. We report results
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of sparse matrices with over 28 mi=
                    llion rows and 640 million non-zero elements
                    acquired from 13 diverse
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">application domains. The experimen=
                    tal results show that our Jacobi parallel
                    implementation on MIC
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">achieves speedups of up to 27.75</= span><!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax==3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"57" style=3D"font-size: 117.4=%; position: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                            <m= jx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-cD7"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-= mo>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">
                            <ma= th xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mo clas=s=3D"MathClass-bin">=C3=97</mo></math>
                        </mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-container>

                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">compared to the sequential implementation=
                        . It also delivers a speedup of up to
                        3.81</span><!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax Ctx=tMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"58" styl=e=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" ar=ia-hidden=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mj=x-cD7"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" d=isplay=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" displa=y=3D"inline">
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=C3=97</mo>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-= mml>
                    </mjx-container>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">compared to a powerful node comprising 24=
                        cores in two Intel Xeon E5-2695v2
                        processors.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Amaral2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-25" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Amaral, V. S. et al. =E2=80=9COn complexity =
            and convergence of high-order coordinate descent
            algorithms=E2=80=9D. In: (Sept. 2020). arXiv: 2009.01811 <span=class=3D"cmtt-10">[math.OC]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Coordinate descent methods with
                high-order regularized models for box-constrained minimizati=
                on
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">are introduced. High-order station=
                    arity asymptotic convergence and first-order
                    stationarity worst-case
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">evaluation complexity bounds are e=
                    stablished. The computer work that is necessary
                    for obtaining
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">first-order </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <= mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex==3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"59" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relati=ve;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-=i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D700 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assis= tive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w=3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>=F0=9D=9C=80</mi>
                        </math></mj= x-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">-stationarity
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">with respect to the variables of e=
                            ach coordinate-descent block is </span>
                        <!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"60" style=3D"font-size=: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"t=rue">
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mj= x-mi>
                                    <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                            <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>

                </= mjx-c>
                </mjx-mo>
                <mjx-mrow>
                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45D=TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mi>
                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">=
                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2B"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mo>
                    <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"=3">
                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mn>
                </mjx-mrow>

                </mjx-mo>
                </mjx-mrow>
                <= mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2F">
                        <= /mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"3">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D=45D TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-clo=se">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>

                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-as= sistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                    xmlns=3D"http://ww=w.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                    <mi>O</mi>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"=MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mi>=F0=9D=9C=80</mi>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin=">=E2=88=92</mo><mrow><mo class=3D"
                                                MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                            <mrow>
                                                <mi>p</mi>
                                                <m= o class=3D"MathClass-bin">+</mo>
                                                    <mn>1</mn>
                                            </mrow>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-clo=se">)</mo>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=95</mo>
                                        <mi>p</mi>
                                        </mrow=>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                    </mrow>
                                </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                </mj= x-container>
                                <span class=3D"cmr-9">whereas the computer work for getting fir=
                                    st-order </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Atta=ched_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"

                                    simultaneously is </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax Ctxt=Menu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"

                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"=></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-op=en">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            <mjx-mrow>
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mj=x-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D700 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mj=x-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mo>=
                                                <mjx-mrow space=3D"3">
                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                                        <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                            <mjx-c clas=s=3D"mjx-c1D45D TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bi=n" space=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2B"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"m=jx-n" space=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mn>
                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                                    <mjx= -mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-= mo>
                                                </mjx-mrow>
                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                                <mjx-c cla=ss=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                    </mjx-math>
                                    <mjx-assistive-mml un=selectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/=Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                            <mi>O</mi>
                                            <mrow>
                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open=">(</mo><mrow><mi>=F0=9D=9C=80</mi><mo class=3D"
                                                    MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</m= o>
                                                    <mrow>
                                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                        <mrow>
                                                            <mi>p</mi>
                                                            <mo class=3D"Mat=hClass-bin">+</mo>
                                                            <mn>1</mn>
                                                        </mrow>
                                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                                        </mr= ow>
                                                    </mrow>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                            </mrow>
                                        </math></mjx-assistive= -mml>
                                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">.
                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Numerical examples involving multi=
                                    dimensional scaling problems are presented. The
                                    numerical performance
                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of the methods is enhanced by mean=
                                    s of coordinate-descent strategies for choosing
                                    initial points.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Anaissi2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-26" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Anaissi, Ali, Basem Suleiman, and Seid Miad =
            Zandavi. =E2=80=9CNeCPD: An Online Tensor
            Decomposition with Optimal Stochastic Gradient Descent=E2=80=9D=
            . In: (Mar. 2020). arXiv:
            2003.08844v1 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[cs.LG]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Multi-way data analysis has
                become an essential tool for capturing underlying structures=
                in
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">higher-order datasets stored in te=
                    nsor </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"63" style=3D"font-size=: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"t=rue">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-sc mjx-b mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4B3 TEX-SC="></mjx-c></mjx-mi><mjx-mo class=3D" mjx-n MathClass-rel"
                                space=3D"4">

                            </mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"4">
                                <= mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c211D TEX-A">
                                    </mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <mjx-msub>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-m= i class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D43C TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        </mjx-= mrow>
                        <mjx-script style=3D"vertical-align: -0.15em; margin-left: -0.064em;">=
                            <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">

                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31">
                                        </mjx= -c>
                                </mjx-mn>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                        </mjx-script>
                        </mjx-msub>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathC=lass-bin" space=3D"3">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-cD7"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>

                            <= /mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">

                                        <mjx-mrow si=ze=3D"s">

                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D441 TEX-I"></mjx-c>=
                                            </mjx-mi>

                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml u=nselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998=/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi mathvariant=3D"bold-script">=F0=9D=92=
                                =B3</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=E2=88=88</mo>
                            <mi>=E2=84=9D</mi>
                            <msub>
                                <= mrow>
                                    <mi>I</mi>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mn>1</mn>
                                    </mrow>
                            </msub>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-=bin">=C3=97</mo>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=E2=8B=AF</mo>
                            <mo class=3D"Math=Class-bin">=C3=97</mo>
                            <msub>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>I</mi>
                                </mrow>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>N</mi>
                                </mrow>
                                <= /msub>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">CANDECOMP/PARAFAC (CP) decompositi=
                    on has been extensively studied and applied to
                    approximate</span>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"64" style=3D"font-size=: 117.4%; position: relative;">

                                TEX-SC="></mjx-c></mjx-mi></mjx-math><mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D" on"
                                displa=y=3D"inline"><math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"
                                    display=3D"=inline">
                                    <mi mathvariant=3D"bold-script">=F0=9D=92=B3</mi>
                                </math></mjx-assis= tive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">by </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container cl=ass=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_c=ounter=3D"65" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">

                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"66" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;="><mjx-math class=3D"
                        MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">

                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                    </mj= x-mn>
                    </mjx-mrow>

                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx=-c29"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mo>
                    </mjx-mrow>
                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-punc">

                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i MathClass-op=" space=3D" 2">
                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2026"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mi>
                    <mjx-mo class=3D"=mjx-n">
                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2061"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mo>
                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n M=athClass-punc">
                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2C"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mo>
                    <mjx-mi class=3D"m=jx-i" space=3D"2">
                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D434 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mi>
                    <mjx-m= row space=3D"2">
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-=c28"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx=-c1D441 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass=-close">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mj= x-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http:=//www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mi>A</mi>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mo class==3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mn>1</mn>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-clo=se">)</mo>
                                </mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-punc">,</mo>
                                <mi class=3D"MathClass-=op">=E2=80=A6</mi>
                                <mo>=E2=81=A1</mo>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-punc">,</mo>
                                <mi>=
                                    A</mi>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mi>N</mi>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mo c=lass=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                </mrow>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-cont= ainer>
                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">where </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-container= class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                ctxtmen=u_counter=3D"67" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                <mjx-math= class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c clas=s=3D"mjx-c1D441 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    </mjx-math>
                                    <mjx-assistive-mml unsel=ectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Mat=h/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                            <mi>N</mi>
                                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                    </mjx-con= tainer>
                                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">represents the order of the tensor. We pr=
                                        opose a new efficient CP decomposition solver
                                        named NeCPD
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">for non-convex problem in multi-wa=
                                        y online data based on stochastic gradient
                                        descent (SGD) algorithm.
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">SGD is very useful in online setti=
                                        ng since it allows us to update </span>
                                    <!-- l. 30 -->
                                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"68" style=3D"font-size=: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"t=rue">
                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-sc mjx-b mjx-i">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4B3 TEX-SC="></mjx-c></mjx-mi><mjx-mrow space=3D"
                                                    2">
                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-o=pen">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"m=jx-i">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D461 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"m=jx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2B"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <= mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-m= n>
                    </mjx-mrow>
                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2=9"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mo>
                    </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable==3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathM=L" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi mathvariant=3D"bold-script">=F0=9D=92=B3</mi>
                            <mro= w>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>t</mi>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass=-bin">+</mo>
                                    <mn>1</mn>
                                </mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                </mrow>
                                </m= ath>
                    </mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">in one single step. In terms of global co=
                    nvergence, it is well known that SGD stuck in
                    many saddle points
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">when it deals with non-convex prob=
                    lems. We study the Hessian matrix to identify
                    theses saddle points,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and then try to escape them using =
                    the perturbation approach which adds little
                    noise to the gradient
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">update step. We further apply Nest=
                    erov=E2=80=99s Accelerated Gradient (NAG) method in SGD
                    algorithm
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">to optimally accelerate the conver=
                    gence rate and compensate Hessian computational
                    delay time per
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">epoch. Experimental evaluation in =
                    the field of structural health monitoring using
                    laboratory-based
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and real-life structural datasets =
                    show that our method provides more accurate
                    results compared with
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">existing online tensor analysis me=
                    thods.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Andrei2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-27" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Andrei, Neculai. <span class=3D"cmti-10">Non=
                linear Conjugate Gradient Methods for
                Unconstrained Optimization</span>.
            Springer International Publishing, 2020. <span class=3D"cmcsc-1=0"><span
                    class=3D"small-caps">doi</span></span>:
            10.1007/978-3-030-42950-8.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Two approaches are known for
                solving large-scale unconstrained optimization problems=E2=
                =80=94the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">limited-memory quasi-Newton method=
                    (truncated Newton method) and the conjugate
                    gradient
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">method. This is the first book to =
                    detail conjugate gradient methods, showing
                    their properties
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and convergence characteristics as=
                    well as their performance in solving
                    large-scale unconstrained
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">optimization problems and applicat=
                    ions. Comparisons to the limited-memory and
                    truncated
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Newton methods are also discussed.=
                    Topics studied in detail include: linear
                    conjugate gradient
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">methods, standard conjugate gradie=
                    nt methods, acceleration of conjugate gradient
                    methods, hybrid,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">modifications of the standard sche=
                    me, memoryless BFGS preconditioned, and
                    three-term. Other
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">conjugate gradient methods with cl=
                    ustering the eigenvalues or with the
                    minimization of the condition
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">number of the iteration matrix, ar=
                    e also treated. For each method, the
                    convergence analysis, the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">computational performances and the=
                    comparisons versus other conjugate gradient
                    methods are given.</span>
                <br class=3D"newline"><span class=3D"cmr-9">The theory behind t=
                    he conjugate gradient algorithms presented as a
                    methodology is developed with
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">a clear, rigorous, and friendly ex=
                    position; the reader will gain an understanding
                    of their properties
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and their convergence and will lea=
                    rn to develop and prove the convergence of
                    his/her own methods.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Numerous numerical studies are sup=
                    plied with comparisons and comments on the
                    behavior of conjugate
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">gradient algorithms for solving a =
                    collection of 800 unconstrained optimization
                    problems of different
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">structures and complexities with t=
                    he number of variables in the range
                    [1000,10000]. The book
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">is addressed to all those interest=
                    ed in developing and using new advanced
                    techniques for solving
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">unconstrained optimization complex=
                    problems. Mathematical programming
                    researchers, theoreticians
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and practitioners in operations re=
                    search, practitioners in engineering and
                    industry researchers, as well
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">as graduate students in mathematic=
                    s, Ph.D. and master students in mathematical
                    programming, will
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">find plenty of information and pra=
                    ctical applications for solving large-scale
                    unconstrained optimization
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">problems and applications by conju=
                    gate gradient methods.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Angriman2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-28" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Angriman, Eugenio et al. =E2=80=9CApproximat=
            ion of the Diagonal of a Laplacian=E2=80=99s Pseudoinverse for
            Complex Network Analysis=E2=80=9D. In: (June 2020). arXiv: 2006=
            .13679v1 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[cs.DS]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">The ubiquity of massive graph
                data sets in numerous applications requires fast algorithms
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">for extracting knowledge from thes=
                    e data. We are motivated here by three
                    electrical measures for the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">analysis of large small-world grap=
                    hs </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0=" jax=3D" CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"69" style=3D"font-size:=117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"tru=e">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D43A TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-= mi>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c3=D"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mrow space=3D"4">
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-=open">

                                </mjx-mo>

                                        </mjx-mi=>

                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29="></mjx-c></mjx-mo></mjx-mrow></mjx-math><mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D=
" on" display=3D"inline"><math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"=display=3D"inline">
                                            <mi>G</mi>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=3D</mo>
                                            <mrow>
                                                <mo=class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                <mrow>

                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-punc"=>,</mo>

                                            </mrow>
                                        </math>
                                        <= /mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">=E2=80=93 i.e., graphs with diameter in <= /span>
                        <!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" ja=x=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"70" style=3D"font-size: 117.=4%; position: relative;">

                                            </mjx-mi>

                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">,
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">which are abundant in complex netw=
                            ork analysis. From a computational point of
                            view, the three
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">measures have in common that their=
                            crucial component is the diagonal of the graph
                            Laplacian=E2=80=99s
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">pseudoinverse, </span>
                        <!-- l. 30 -=
-><mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabind=

p://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline"><mi>L</mi><mo class=3D"=

an class=3D"cmr-9">.
            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Computing diag</span><!-- l. 30 --=

ive;"><mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true"><mjx-mrow><mjx-mo cl=

            <span class=3D"cmr-9">exactly by pseudoinversion, however, is a=
s expensive as dense matrix multiplication =E2=80=93
               and the standard
            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">tools in practice even require cub=
ic time. Moreover, the pseudoinverse requires
               quadratic space =E2=80=93
            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">hardly feasible for large graphs. =
Resorting to approximation by, e.g., using the
               Johnson-Lindenstrauss
            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">transform, requires the solution o=
f </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0"=jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"73" style=3D"font-size: 1=17.4%; position: relative;">
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true="><mjx-mi class=3D" mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-m= i>
                                <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                        <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mo>
                                    <mjx-mrow>
                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-n qopname">
                                            <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c6C">
                                                </mjx-c>
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6F"></mjx-c>
                                                <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c67"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                            <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c2061"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
