                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        </m= jx-mi>
                                </mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">=
                                    <math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>n=
                                        </mi>
                                    </math>
                                </mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-container>
                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">rank one sparse matrices, whose nonzero e=
                                ntries, under the appropriate permutations,
                                form striped
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">blocks - we therefore refer to the=
                                m as Permuted Striped Block (PSB) matrices. We
                                define the </span><span class=3D"cmti-9">PSB
                            </span><span class=3D"cmti-9">data model </span><span class=3D"=cmr-9">as a particular
                                distribution over this
                                class of matrices, motivated by its implications for
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">community detection, provable bina=
                                ry dictionary learning with real valued sparse
                                coding, and blind
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">combinatorial compressed sensing. =
                                For data matrices drawn from the PSB data
                                model, we provide
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">computationally efficient factoriz=
                                ation algorithms which recover the generating
                                factors with high
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">probability from as few as </span>=
                            <!-- l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"C=HTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                ctxtmenu_counter=3D"460" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; p=osition: relative;">
                                <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                    <mjx-m= i class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D441 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                        <mjx-m= o class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c3D">
                                                </mjx-= c>
                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"4">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 T=EX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                <mjx-mfenced>
                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop">
                                                        <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c28 TEX-S1"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-mfrac>
                                                            <mjx-frac>
                                                                <mjx-nu= m>
                                                                    <mjx-nstrut></mjx-nstrut>
                                                                    <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                            <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I">
                                            </mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                            </mjx-num>
                                            <mjx-d= box>
                                                <mjx-dtable>
                                                    <mjx-line></mjx-line>
                                                    <mjx-row>
                                                        <mjx-den>
                                                            <mjx-dstrut>
                                                                </mjx-ds= trut>
                                                                <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45=8 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                                                </mjx-mrow>
                                                        </mjx-den>
                                                    </mjx-row>
                                                </mjx-dtable>
                                                </mjx= -dbox>
                                                </mjx-frac>
                                                </mjx-mfrac>
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-n qopname">
                                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c6C"></mjx-c>
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6F"></mjx-c>
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c=67"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mj=x-c2061"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32">=
                                                    </mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mn>
                                                <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-ope=n">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                                    <mjx-mo c=lass=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    </= mjx-mrow>
                                                </mjx-mrow>
                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29 TEX-S=1"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                </mjx-mfenced>
                                </mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml unselectabl=e=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/Math=ML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>N</mi>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=3D</mo>
                                        <mi>O<= /mi>
                                                <mfenced open=3D"(" separators=3D"" close=3D")">
                                                    <mrow>
                                                        <mfrac>
                                                            <mrow>
                                                                <mi>=
                                                                    n</mi>
                                                            </mrow>
                                                            <mrow>
                                                                <mi>k</mi>
                                                            </mrow>
                                                        </mfrac>
                                                        <mi class=3D"qopname">log</mi>
                                                        <= mo>=E2=81=A1</mo>
                                                            <mn>2</mn>
                                                            <mrow>
                                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                                <mrow>
                                                                    <= mi>n</mi>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                        </mrow>
                                        </mrow>
                                        </mfenced=>

                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">data vectors, where </span><!-- l. 30 -->=

                                ctxtmenu_counter=3D"461" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relat=ive;">
                                <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assi= stive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        xmlns=3D"http://www.=w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>k</mi>
                                    </math></mjx-assistiv= e-mml>
                            </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">,</span>
                            <!-- l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                ctxtmenu_counter=3D"462" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45A TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        </m= jx-mi>
                                </mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">=
                                    <math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>m=
                                        </mi>
                                    </math>
                                </mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-container>
                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">and </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-container c=lass=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                ctxtmenu_=counter=3D"463" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                <mjx-math=class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml unsele=ctable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math=/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>n</mi>
                                    </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                </mjx-cont= ainer>
                                <span class=3D"cmr-9">scale proportionally. Notably, these algo=
                                    rithms achieve optimal sample complexity up to
                                    logarithmic
                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">factors.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Myers2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-331" class=3D"thebibliography">

        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Myers, Jeremy M. et al. =E2=80=9CParameter S=
            ensitivity Analysis of the SparTen High Performance
            Sparse Tensor Decomposition Software: Extended Analysis=E2=80=
            =9D. In: (Dec. 2020). arXiv: 2012.01520
            <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[math.NA]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Tensor decomposition models play
                an increasingly important role in modern data science
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">applications. One problem of parti=
                    cular interest is fitting a low-rank Canonical
                    Polyadic (CP) tensor
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">decomposition model when the tenso=
                    r has sparse structure and the tensor elements
                    are nonnegative
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">count data. SparTen is a high-perf=
                    ormance C++ library which computes a low-rank
                    decomposition
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">using different solvers: a first-o=
                    rder quasi-Newton or a second-order damped
                    Newton method, along
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">with the appropriate choice of run=
                    time parameters. Since default parameters in
                    SparTen are tuned to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">experimental results in prior publ=
                    ished work on a single real-world dataset
                    conducted using MATLAB
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">implementations of these methods, =
                    it remains unclear if the parameter defaults in
                    SparTen are
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">appropriate for general tensor dat=
                    a. Furthermore, it is unknown how sensitive
                    algorithm convergence is
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">to changes in the input parameter =
                    values. This report addresses these unresolved
                    issues with large-scale
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">experimentation on three benchmark=
                    tensor data sets. Experiments were conducted
                    on several different
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">CPU architectures and replicated w=
                    ith many initial states to establish
                    generalized profiles of algorithm
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">convergence behavior.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Navarro2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-332" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Navarro, Crist=C3=B3bal A. et al. =E2=80=9CG=
            PU Tensor Cores for fast Arithmetic Reductions=E2=80=9D. In: (Jan.
            2020). arXiv: 2001.05585 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[cs.DC]</span>=
            .
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">This work proposes a GPU tensor
                core approach that encodes the arithmetic reduction of</span=>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"464" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </m= jx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">=
                        <math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>n=
                            </mi>
                        </math>
                    </mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">numbers as a set of chained </span>
                <!-- l=
. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"=tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"465" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; positio=n: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi clas=s=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45A TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo clas=s=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-cD7"></mjx-c>
                            </mj= x-mo>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"3">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45A TEX-I">=
                                </mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display==3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"i=nline">
                            <mi>m</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=C3=97</mo>
                            <mi>m</mi>
                        </math></= mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">matrix multiply accumulate (MMA) operatio=
                    ns executed in parallel by GPU tensor cores.
                    The asymptotic
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">running time of the proposed chain=
                    ed tensor core approach is </span>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"466" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D447 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </m= jx-mi>
                            <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                    <mjx-c c=lass=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c=class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx=-n MathClass-close">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            <m= jx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c3D">
                                    </= mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mo>
                                    <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"4">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c35"=></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mn>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D459 TEX-I"=></mjx-c>
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45C TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    <mjx-msub>
                                        <mjx-= mrow>
                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D454 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                </mj= x-mi>
                                                </mjx-mrow>
                                                <mjx-script style=3D"vertical-align: -0.15em;">
                                                    <mjx-mrow si=ze=3D"s">
                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45A TEX-I"></mjx-c>=
                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mn>=
                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                                </mjx-script>
                                    </mjx-msub>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"=mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectab=le=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/Mat=hML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>T</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>=
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>n</mi>
                                </mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                            </mrow>
                            <mo class==3D"MathClass-rel">=3D</mo>
                            <mn>5</mn>
                            <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">lo</mi>
                            <msu= b>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>g</mi>
                                </mrow>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>m</mi>
                                    <mn>2</mn>
                                </mrow>
                                </msub>
                                <mi>n</mi>=
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">and its speedup is </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <= mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex==3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"467" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relat=ive;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D446 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx=-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c3D"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mj= x-mfrac space=3D"4">
                            <mjx-frac>
                                <mjx-num>
                                    <mjx-nstrut></mjx-nstrut>
                                    <mjx-mrow s=ize=3D"s">
                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c34"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mn=>
                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                </mjx-num>
                                <mjx-dbox>
                                    <mjx-dtable>
                                        <mjx-line></mjx-line>
                                        <mjx-row>
                                            <= mjx-den>
                                                <mjx-dstrut></mjx-dstrut>
                                                <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                    <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-=n">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c35"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mn>
                                                </mjx-mrow>
                                                </mjx-den>
                                                </mjx-row=>
                                    </mjx-dtable>
                                </mjx-dbox>
                            </mjx-frac>
                            </mjx-mfrac>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c1D459 TEX-I">
                                    </mjx-c>
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45C TEX-I">
                </= mjx-c>
                </mjx-mi>
                <mjx-msub>
                    <mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"=mjx-c1D454 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-mrow>
                    <mjx-script style=3D"vertical=-align: -0.15em;">
                        <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                            <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mn>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-script>
                </mjx-msub>
                <mjx-mi cl=ass=3D"mjx-i">
                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45A TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                </mjx-mi>
                <mjx-mn cl=ass=3D"mjx-n">
                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                </mjx-mn>
                </mjx-math>
                <mjx-ass= istive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                        xmlns=3D"http://www=.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                        <mi>S</mi>
                        <mo class=3D"MathCla=ss-rel">=3D</mo>
                        <mfrac>
                            <mrow>
                                <mn>4</mn>
                            </mrow>
                            <mrow>
                                <mn>5</mn>
                            </mrow>
                            </mfra= c>
                            <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">lo</mi>
                            <msub>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>g</mi>
                                </mrow>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mn>=
                                        2</mn>
                                </mrow>
                            </msub>
                            <mi>m</mi>
                            <mn>2</mn>
                    </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-co= ntainer>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">over the classic </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mj= x-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D="0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"468" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative=;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i"=>
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                <= mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                    </mj= x-mo>
                    <mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I">
                                </= mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-n qopname" space=3D"2">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"=mjx-c6C"></mjx-c>
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6F"></mjx-c>
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c67">=
                            </mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2=061"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TE=X-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-mrow>
                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                        <m= jx-c class=3D"mjx-c29">
                            </mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mo>
                    </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistiv= e-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.o=rg/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>O</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathCl=ass-open">(</mo>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>n</mi>
                                    <mi class=3D"qopname">log</mi>
                                    <mo>=E2=81=A1=
                                    </mo>
                                    <mi>n</mi>
                                </mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                            </mrow>
                        </math></m= jx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container>
                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">parallel reduction algorithm. Experimenta=
                            l performance results show that the proposed
                            reduction
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">method is </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mj= x-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D="0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"469" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative=;">
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n=MathClass-rel">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c223C"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mn class=3D="mjx-n" space=3D"4">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c33"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mn>
                                <mjx-mo class==3D"mjx-n MathClass-punc">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2E"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mn= class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mn>
                                    <mjx= -mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-cD7"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo=>
                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">
                                <math= xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                    <mo class==3D"MathClass-rel">=E2=88=BC</mo>
                                    <mn>3</mn>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-punc">.</= mo>
                                        <mn>2</mn>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=C3=97</mo></math></mjx-assistive-= mml>
                                        </mjx-container>
                                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">faster than a conventional GPU reduction =
                                            implementation, and preserves the numerical
                                            precision
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">because the sub-results of each ch=
                                            ain of </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attach=ed_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                                            tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"470" style=3D"font-s=ize: 117.4%;
                                            position: relative;">
                                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden==3D"true">
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D445 TEX-I">
                                                        </mjx-c=>
                                                </mjx-mi>
                                            </mjx-math>
                                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inli=ne"><math
                                                    xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                                    <= mi>R</mi>
                                                </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                        </mjx-container>
                                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">MMAs is kept as a 32-bit floating point v=
                                            alue, before being all reduced into as a final
                                            32-bit result. The
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">chained MMA design allows a flexib=
                                            le configuration of thread-blocks; small
                                            thread-blocks of 32 or 128
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">threads can still achieve maximum =
                                            performance using a chain of </span>
                                        <!-- l. 30 -->
                                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                                            tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"471" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%;
                                            position: relative;">
                                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D445 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    </m= jx-mi>
                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx=-c3D"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"4">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"=mjx-c34"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mn>
                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-punc">
                                                        <mjx-c cla=ss=3D"mjx-c2C"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                                        <mjx-c=class=3D"mjx-c35"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mn>
                                            </mjx-math>
                                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectab=le=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                    xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/Mat=hML" display=3D"inline">
                                                    <mi>R</mi>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=3D</mo>
                                                    <mn>4=
                                                    </mn>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-punc">,</mo>
                                                    <mn>5</mn>
                                                </math></mjx-assistive-mm= l>
                                        </mjx-container>
                                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">MMAs per block, while large thread-blocks=
                                            work best with </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMe=nu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                                            tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"472" style==3D"font-size: 117.4%;
                                            position: relative;">
                                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" ari=a-hidden=3D"true">
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D445 TEX-I"=></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                                                    <mjx-c=class=3D"mjx-c3D"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"4">
                                                    <mjx= -c class=3D"mjx-c31">
                                                        </mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mn>
                                            </mjx-math>
                                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselec=table=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                    xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/=MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                                    <mi>R</mi>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=3D</mo>
                                                    <m= n>1</mn>
                                                </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">.
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">The results obtained in this work =
                                            show that tensor cores can indeed provide a
                                            significant performance
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">improvement to non-Machine Learnin=
                                            g applications such as the arithmetic
                                            reduction, which is an
                                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">integration tool for studying many=
                                            scientific phenomena.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nayak2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-333" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Nayak, Pratik, Terry Cojean, and Hartwig Anz=
            t. =E2=80=9CEvaluating Abstract Asynchronous Schwarz
            solvers=E2=80=9D. In: (Mar. 2020). arXiv: 2003.05361v1 <span cl=ass=3D"cmtt-10">[cs.DC]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">With the commencement of the
                exascale computing era, we realize that the majority of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the leadership supercomputers are =
                    heterogeneous and massively parallel even on a
                    single node with
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">multiple co-processors such as GPU=
                    s and multiple cores on each node. For example,
                    ORNLs Summit
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">accumulates six NVIDIA Tesla V100s=
                    and 42 core IBM Power9s on each node.
                    Synchronizing across
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">all these compute resources in a s=
                    ingle node or even across multiple nodes is
                    prohibitively expensive.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Hence it is necessary to develop a=
                    nd study asynchronous algorithms that
                    circumvent this issue of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">bulk-synchronous computing for mas=
                    sive parallelism. In this study, we examine the
                    asynchronous
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">version of the abstract Restricted=
                    Additive Schwarz method as a solver where we
                    do not explicitly
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">synchronize, but allow for communi=
                    cation of the data between the sub-domains to
                    be completely
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">asynchronous thereby removing the =
                    bulk synchronous nature of the algorithm. We
                    accomplish this
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">by using the onesided RMA function=
                    s of the MPI standard. We study the benefits of
                    using such an

                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">asynchronous solver over its synch=
                    ronous counterpart on both multi-core
                    architectures and on multiple
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">GPUs. We also study the communicat=
                    ion patterns and local solvers and their effect
                    on the global
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">solver. Finally, we show that this=
                    concept can render attractive runtime benefits
                    over the synchronous
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">counterparts.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nayak2020a" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-334" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">=E2=80=94 =E2=80=9CEvaluating asynchronous S=
            chwarz solvers on GPUs=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">The
                International Journal
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">of High Performance Computing Ap=
                plications </span>(Aug. 2020), p.
            109434202094681. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-=caps">doi</span></span>:
            10.1177/1094342020946814.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">With the commencement of the
                exascale computing era, we realize that the majority of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the leadership supercomputers are =
                    heterogeneous and massively parallel. Even a
                    single node can
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">contain multiple co-processors suc=
                    h as GPUs and multiple CPU cores. For example,
                    ORNL=E2=80=99s Summit
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">accumulates six NVIDIA Tesla V100 =
                    GPUs and 42 IBM Power9 cores on each node.
                    Synchronizing
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">across compute resources of multip=
                    le nodes can be prohibitively expensive. Hence,
                    it is necessary to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">develop and study asynchronous alg=
                    orithms that circumvent this issue of
                    bulk-synchronous computing.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">In this study, we examine the asyn=
                    chronous version of the abstract Restricted
                    Additive Schwarz method
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">as a solver. We do not explicitly =
                    synchronize, but allow the communication
                    between the sub-domains
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">to be completely asynchronous, the=
                    reby removing the bulk synchronous nature of
                    the algorithm.</span><br class=3D"newline"><span class=3D"cm=r-9">We accomplish this by using the
                    one-sided
                    Remote Memory Access (RMA) functions of the MPI
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">standard. We study the benefits of=
                    using such an asynchronous solver over its
                    synchronous counterpart.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">We also study the communication pa=
                    tterns governed by the partitioning and the
                    overlap between the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">sub-domains on the global solver. =
                    Finally, we show that this concept can render
                    attractive performance
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">benefits over the synchronous coun=
                    terparts even for a well-balanced
                    problem.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nayak2020b" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-335" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">=E2=80=94 =E2=80=9CTwo-stage Asynchronous It=
            erative Solversfor multi-GPU Clusters=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10"=>Proceedings of the
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">11th IEEE/ACM Workshop on Latest=
                Advances in Scalable Algorithms for
                Large-Scale Systems</span>.
            ScalA =E2=80=9920. 2020.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Given the trend of supercomputers
                accumulating much of their compute power in
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">GPU accelerators composed of thous=
                    ands of cores and operating in streaming mode,
                    global
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">synchronization points become a bo=
                    ttleneck, severely confining the performance of
                    applications.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">In consequence, asynchronous metho=
                    ds breaking up the bulk-synchronous programming
                    model are
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">becoming increasingly attractive. =
                    In this paper, we study a GPU-focused
                    asynchronous version of the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Restricted Additive Schwarz (RAS) =
                    method that employs preconditioned Krylov
                    subspace methods as
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">subdomain solvers. We analyze the =
                    method for various parameters such as local
                    solver tolerance and
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">iteration counts. Leveraging the m=
                    ulti-GPU architecture on Summit, we show that
                    these two-stage
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">methods are more memory and time e=
                    fficient than asynchronous RAS using direct
                    solvers. We
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">also demonstrate the superiority o=
                    ver synchronous counterparts, and present
                    results using one-sided
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">CUDA-aware MPI on up to 36 NVIDIA =
                    V100 GPUs.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nesi2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-336" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Nesi, Lucas Leandro et al. <span class=3D"cm=ti-10">Distributed-memory multi-GPU
                block-sparse
                tensor contraction for
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">electronic structure</span>. Tec=
            h. rep. Universit=C3=A9 Grenoble Alpes, CNRS, Inria,
            2020. HAL: 02960848.
            <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">url</span>
                <= /span>: <a href=3D"https://hal.inria.fr/hal-02960848/document" class=3D"url="><span class=3D"
                        cmtt-10">https://hal.inria.fr/hal-02960848/document
            </span>=
            </a>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">High-performance computing (HPC)
                applications enable the solution of computeintensive
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">problems in feasible time. Among m=
                    any HPC paradigms, task-based programming has
                    gathered
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">community attention in recent year=
                    s. This paradigm enables constructing an HPC
                    application using a
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">more declarative approach, structu=
                    ring it in a direct acyclic graph (DAG). The
                    performance evaluation
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of these applications is as hard a=
                    s in any other programming paradigm.
                    Understanding how to analyze
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">these applications, employing the =
                    DAG and runtime metrics, presents opportunities
                    to improve its
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">performance. This article describe=
                    s the StarVZ R-package available on CRAN for
                    performance analysis
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of task-based applications. StarVZ=
                    enables transforms runtime trace data into
                    different visualizations
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of the application behavior. An an=
                    alyst can understand their applications=E2=80=99
                    performance limitations and

                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">compare multiple executions. StarV=
                    Z has been successfully applied to several
                    study-cases, showing its
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">applicability in a number of scena=
                    rios.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nesterov2020a" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-337" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Nesterov, Yurii. <span class=3D"cmti-10">Ine=
                xact Accelerated High-Order Proximal-Point
                Methods</span>. Tech. rep. UCL
            - SSH/LIDAM/CORE - Center for operations research and econometr=
            ics, 2020. CORE
            Discussion Papers: 2020/08. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span clas=s=3D"small-caps">url</span></span>: <a
                href=3D"http://hdl.handle.net/2078.1=/227219" class=3D"url"><span
                    class=3D"cmtt-10">http://hdl.handle.net/2078.1=
                    /227219</span></a>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper, we present a new
                framework of Bi-Level Unconstrained Minimization (BLUM)
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">for development of accelerated met=
                    hods in Convex Programming. These methods use
                    approximations
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of the high-order proximal points,=
                    which are solutions of some auxiliary
                    parametric optimization
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">problems. For computing these poin=
                    ts, we can use different methods, and, in
                    particular, the lower-order
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">schemes. This opens a possibility =
                    for the latter methods to overpass traditional
                    limits of the Complexity
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Theory. As an example, we obtain a=
                    new second-order method with the convergence
                    rate </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax Ct=xtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"473" st=yle=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX"=aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX=-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass=-open">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mi class=3D="mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mi>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D="mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-= mo>
                                    <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c34"></mjx-c>
                                        </m= jx-mn>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mj=x-c29"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectab=le=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/Mat=hML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>O</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>=
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>k</mi>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                    <mn>4</mn>
                                </mrow>=
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                            </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx= -container>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">, where </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-contain= er class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtm=enu_counter=3D"474" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                        <mjx-m= ath class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c c=lass=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-assistive-mml un=selectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                    xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/=Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                    <mi>k</mi>
                                </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-= container>
                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the iteration counter. This rate is be=
                                tter than the maximal possible rate of
                                convergence for this type of
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">methods, as applied to functions w=
                                ith Lipschitz continuous Hessian. We also
                                present new methods with
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the exact auxiliary search procedu=
                                re, which have the rate of convergence </span>
                            <!-- l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                ctxtmenu_counter=3D"475" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        </m= jx-mi>
                                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                                <mjx-c c=lass=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            <mjx-mrow>
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c=class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClas=s-bin" space=3D"3">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                <mjx-mrow sp=ace=3D"3">
                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28">
                                                            <= /mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c33">=
                                                            </mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mn>
                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45D TEX-I">=
                                                            </mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c c=lass=3D"mjx-c2B"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-= c class=3D"mjx-c31">
                                                                </mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mn>
                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n Math=Class-close">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                </mjx-mrow>
                                                <mjx-mo c=lass=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2F"></mjx-c>
                                                    <= /mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32">
                                                                </mjx-= c>
                                                        </mjx-mn>
                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                                <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                </mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml unse=lectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Ma=th/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>O</mi>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">=
                                                (</mo>
                                            <mrow>
                                                <mi>k</mi>
                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                                <mrow>
                                                    <mo c=lass=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                    <mrow>
                                                        <mn>3</mn>
                                                        <mi>p</mi>
                                                        <mo class=3D"MathCl=ass-bin">+</mo>
                                                        <mn>1</mn>
                                                    </mrow>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                                </mrow>=
                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=95</mo>
                                                <mn>2</mn>
                                            </mrow>
                                            <mo class=3D"Mat=hClass-close">)</mo>
                                        </mrow>
                                    </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-container>
                            <span= class=3D"cmr-9">,
                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">where </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                <mjx-co= ntainer class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                                    tabindex=3D"0"=ctxtmenu_counter=3D"476" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position:
                                    relative;">
                                    <= mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                            <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45D TEX-I">
                                                </mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n Math=Class-rel" space=3D"4">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2265"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        <mjx-mn=class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"4">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mn>
                                        </mjx= -math>
                                        <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                xmlns==3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                                <mi>p</mi>
                                                <mo cl=ass=3D"MathClass-rel">=E2=89=A5</mo>
                                                <mn>1</mn>
                                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                    </= mjx-container>
                                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the order of the proximal operator. Th=
                                        e auxiliary problem at each iteration of these
                                        schemes is
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">convex.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nesterov2020c" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-338" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">=E2=80=94 =E2=80=9CInexact basic tensor meth=
            ods for
            some classes of convex optimization problems=E2=80=9D. In: <spa= n class=3D"cmti-10">Optimization Methods
                and Software
                </span>(Dec.
                2020), pp. 1=E2=80=9329. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class==3D"small-caps">doi</span></span>:
                10.1080/10556788.2020.1854252.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper, we analyse the
                Basic Tensor Methods, which use approximate solutions of the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">auxiliary problems. The quality of=
                    this solution is described by the residual in
                    thefunction value, which
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">must be proportional to </span>
                <!-= - l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTM=L" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"477" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; posi=tion: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mi c=lass=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <mjx-mfra= c>
                                <mjx-frac>
                                    <mjx-num>
                                        <mjx-nstrut></mjx-nstrut>
                                        <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                            <mjx-mi=class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45D TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                            <mjx-mo=class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2B"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            <mj= x-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mn>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                        <= /mjx-num>
                                            <mjx-dbox>
                                                <mjx-dtable>
                                                    <mjx-line></mjx-line>
                                                    <mjx-row>
                                                        <mjx-den>
                                                            <mjx-= dstrut>
                                                                </mjx-dstrut>
                                                                <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                        <mjx-c cla=ss=3D"mjx-c1D45D TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                                                </mjx-mrow>
                                                        </mjx-den>
                                                    </mjx-row>
                                                    </m= jx-dtable>
                                            </mjx-dbox>
                                </mjx-frac>
                                </mjx-mfrac>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assistive-mml u=nselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998=/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                                <mfrac>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mi>p</m= i>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">+</mo>
                                            <mn>1</mn>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mi>p</mi>
                                        </mrow=>
                                </mfrac>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">,
                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">where </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-co= ntainer class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                        tabindex=3D"0"=ctxtmenu_counter=3D"478" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                        <= mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45D TEX-I">
                                    </mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n Math=Class-rel" space=3D"4">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2265"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mn=class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"4">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mn>
                            </mjx= -math>
                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                    xmlns==3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                    <mi>p</mi>
                                    <mo cl=ass=3D"MathClass-rel">=E2=89=A5</mo>
                                    <mn>1</mn>
                                </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                        </= mjx-container>
                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the order of the method and </span>
                        <!-= - l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTM=L" tabindex=3D"0"
                                ctxtmenu_counter=3D"479" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; posi=tion: relative;">
                                <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                    <mjx-mi c=lass=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    </mjx-mat= h>
                                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                            xmlns=3D"=http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                            <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi=>
                                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-container>
                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the desired accuracy in the main optim=
                                ization problem. We analyse in details the
                                auxiliary schemes
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">for the third- and second-order te=
                                nsor methods. The auxiliary problems for the
                                third-order scheme can
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">be solved very efficiently by a li=
                                nearly convergent gradient-type method with a
                                preconditioner. The
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">most expensive operation in this p=
                                rocess is a preliminary factorization of the
                                Hessian of the objective
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">function. For solving the auxiliar=
                                y problem for the second order scheme, we
                                suggest two variants of the
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Fast Gradient Methods with restart=
                                , which converge as </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax Ct=xtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                ctxtmenu_counter=3D"480" st=yle=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX"=aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX=-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop MathCla=ss-open">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28 TEX-S1"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-mf= rac>
                                                <mjx-frac>
                                                    <mjx-num>
                                                        <mjx-nstrut></mjx-nstrut>
                                                        <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                            <mjx-m= n class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mn>
                                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                                        </mj= x-num>
                                                        <mjx-dbox>
                                                            <mjx-dtable>
                                                                <mjx-line></mjx-line>
                                                                <mjx-row>
                                                                    <mjx-den>
                                                                        <mjx-dst= rut>
                                                                            </mjx-dstrut>
                                                                            <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I">
                                                                                    </mjx-c>
                                                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c36"></mjx-c>
                                                                                </mjx-mn>
                                                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                                                    </mjx-den>
                                                                </mjx-row>
                                                            </mjx-dtable>
                                                            <= /mjx-dbox>
                                                </mjx-frac>
                                                </mjx-mfrac>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop MathCl=ass-close">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29 TEX-S1"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                    </mj= x-math>
