                                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                            xmln=s=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                            <mi>O</mi>
                                            <mrow=>
                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                <mrow>
                                                    <mfrac>
                                                        <mrow>
                                                            <mn>1</mn>
                                                        </mrow>
                                                        <mr= ow>
                                                            <mi>k</mi>
                                                            <mn>6</mn>
                                                </mrow>
                                                </mfrac>
                                                </mrow>
                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">=
                                                    )</mo>
                                                </mrow>
                                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-=9">,
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">where </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-co= ntainer class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                                tabindex=3D"0"=ctxtmenu_counter=3D"481" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position:
                                relative;">
                                <= mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I">
                                            </mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    </mjx-math>
                                    <mjx-assistive-m= ml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/=1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                            <mi>k</mi>
                                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                        <= /mjx-container>
                                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the iteration counter. Finally, we pre=
                                                sent the results of the preliminary
                                                computational experiments.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nesterov2020b" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-339" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">=E2=80=94 <span class=3D"cmti-10">Inexact hi=
                gh-order proximal-point methods with auxiliary search
                procedure</span>. Tech. rep.
            UCL - SSH/LIDAM/CORE - Center for operations research and econo=
            metrics, 2020. CORE
            Discussion Papers: 2020/10. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span clas=s=3D"small-caps">url</span></span>: <a
                href=3D"http://hdl.handle.net/2078.1=/227954" class=3D"url"><span
                    class=3D"cmtt-10">http://hdl.handle.net/2078.1=
                    /227954</span></a>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper, we complement the
                framework of Bi-Level Unconstrained Minimization (BLUM)[21]
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">by a new pth-order proximal-point =
                    method convergent as </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax C=txtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"482" s=tyle=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX"=aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TE=X-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClas=s-open">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mi class==3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mi>
                                <mjx-mo class==3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212"></mjx-c>
                                    </m= jx-mo>
                                    <mjx-mrow space=3D"3">
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                            <mjx-c c=lass=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                <mjx-c=class=3D"mjx-c33"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mn>
                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"=mjx-c1D45D TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" sp=ace=3D"3">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2B"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n"=space=3D"3">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mn>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                        <mjx-mo c=lass=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        </= mjx-mrow>
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"=mjx-c2F"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                            <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mn>
                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-c=lose">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mo>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-= assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://=www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>O</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class==3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>k</mi>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=
                                        =88=92</mo>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mn>3</mn>
                                            <mi>p</= mi>
                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">+</mo>
                                                <mn>1</mn>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClas=s-close">)</mo>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=95</mo>
                                    <mn>2</mn>
                                    <= /mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml=>
                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">where k is the iteration counter. =
                    As compared with [21], we replace the auxiliary
                    line search by a convex
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">segment search. This allows us to =
                    bound its complexity of by a logarithm of the
                    desired accuracy.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Each step in this search needs an =
                    approximate computation of the proximal-point
                    operator. Under
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">assumption on boundedness of
            </spa= n><!-- l. 30 -->
            <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D="CHTML" tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"483"
                style=3D"font-size: 117.4%;=position: relative;">
                <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                    <mjx= -mrow>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28">
                    </mjx= -c>
                    </mjx-mo>
                    <mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45D TE=X-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                            <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c2B">
                                </mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">=
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mn>
                    </mjx-mrow>
                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n=MathClass-close">
                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                    </mjx-mo>
                    </mjx-mrow>
                    </mj= x-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmln=s=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class==3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>p</mi>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">+</mo=>
                                        <mn>1</mn>
                                </mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                            </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-a= ssistive-mml>
            </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">st
            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">derivative of the objective functi=
                on, this can be done by one step of the
                pth-order augmented tensor
            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">method. In this way, for </span>
            <!= -- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHT=ML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"484" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; pos=ition: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi=class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45D TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo=class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c3D"></mjx-c>=
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"4">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32">
                                </mjx= -c>
                        </mjx-mn>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"in=line"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline"=>
                            <mi>p</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=3D</mo>
                            <mn>2</mn>
                        </math></mjx-assis= tive-mml>
                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">we get a new second-order method w=
                    ith the rate of convergence </span>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"485" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </m= jx-mi>
                            <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                    <mjx-c c=lass=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c=class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClas=s-bin" space=3D"3">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mo>
                                    <mjx-mn clas=s=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c37"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mn>
                                    <mjx-mo c=lass=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2F"></mjx-c>
                                        <= /mjx-mo>
                                            <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32">
                                                    </mjx-= c>
                                            </mjx-mn>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unse=lectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Ma=th/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>O</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">=
                                    (</mo>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>k</mi>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                    <mn>7</mn>
                                    <= mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=95</mo>
                                        <mn>2</mn>
                                </mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"Math=Class-close">)</mo>
                            </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">and logarithmic complexity of the auxilia=
                    ry search at each iteration. Another
                    possibility is to compute

                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the proximal-point operator by low=
                    er-order minimization methods. As an example,
                    for </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax Ctx=tMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"486" sty=le=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" a=ria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45D TEX-=I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                            <mjx-= c class=3D"mjx-c3D">
                                </mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"4">
                            <m= jx-c class=3D"mjx-c33">
                                </mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mn>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unsel=ectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Mat=h/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>p</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=3D</mo>=
                            <mn>3</mn>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">=
                    ,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">we consider the upper-level proces=
                    s convergent as </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMe=nu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"487" style==3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" ari=a-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"=></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-op=en">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mj=x-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mi>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mj=x-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>=
                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c35"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-= mn>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c=29"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable==3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathM=L" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>O</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                <m= row>
                                    <mi>k</mi>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                    <mn>5</mn>
                            </mrow>
                            <m= o class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-c= ontainer><span class=3D"cmr-9">.
                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Assuming the boundedness of fourth=
                        derivative, an appropriate approximation of
                        the proximal-point
                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">operator can be computed by a seco=
                        nd-order method in logarithmic number of
                        iterations. This combination
                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">gives a second-order scheme with m=
                        uch better complexity than the existing
                        theoretical limits.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nesterov2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-340" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">=E2=80=94 <span class=3D"cmti-10">Superfast =
                second-order methods for Unconstrained Convex
                Optimization</span>. Tech. rep. UCL -
            SSH/LIDAM/CORE - Center for operations research and econometric=
            s, 2020. CORE Discussion
            Papers: 2020/07. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-=caps">url</span></span>: <a
                href=3D"https://dial.uclouvain.be/pr/boreal/obj=ect/boreal%3A227146/datastream/PDF_01/view"
                class=3D"url"><span
                    class=3D"cm=tt-10">https://dial.uclouvain.be/pr/boreal/object/boreal%3A227146/datastrea=
                    m/PDF_01/view</span></a>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper, we present new
                second-order methods with converge rate </span>
                <!-- l. 30 --=
><mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabinde=
x=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"488" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: rela=
tive;"><mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true"><mjx-mo class=3D"mj=
x-n MathClass-bin"><mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c4F"></mjx-c><mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28=
"></mjx-c><mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6B"></mjx-c><mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212"></mjx=
-c><mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c34"></mjx-c><mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c></mjx-=
mo></mjx-math><mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><ma=
th xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline"><mo clas=
s=3D"MathClass-bin">O(k=E2=88=924)</mo></math></mjx-assistive-mml></mjx-con=
tainer><span class=3D"cmr-9">,
            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">where </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-co= ntainer class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                    tabindex=3D"0"=ctxtmenu_counter=3D"489" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <= mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I">
                                </mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assistive-m= ml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/=1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mi>k</mi>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                            <= /mjx-container>
                                <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the iteration counter. This is faster =
                                    that the existing lower bound for this type of
                                    schemes [1, 2], which
                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">is </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                <mjx-conta= iner class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                    ctx=tmenu_counter=3D"490" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                    <mjx= -math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                            <mjx-c= class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I">
                                                </mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                            <mjx-mo=class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            <m= jx-mrow>
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    <= /mjx-mi>
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"m=jx-c2212"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c37"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mn>
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space==3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2F"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" sp=ace=3D"3">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mn>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                        <mjx-mo clas=s=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                    </mjx= -mrow>
                                    </mjx-math>
                                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">=
                                        <math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                            <mi>O=
                                            </mi>
                                            <mrow>
                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                <mrow>
                                                    <mi>k</mi>
                                                    <mo class=3D"=MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                                    <mn>7</mn>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=
                                                        =95</mo>
                                                    <mn>2</mn>
                                                </mrow>
                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                            </mrow>
                                        </math>=
                                    </mjx-assistive-mml>
                                    </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">.
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Our progress can be explained by a=
                                        finer specification of the problem class. The
                                        main idea of this
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">approach consists in implementatio=
                                        n of the third-order scheme from [15] using the
                                        second-order oracle.
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">At each iteration of our method, w=
                                        e solve a nontrivial auxiliary problem by a
                                        linearly convergent scheme
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">based on the relative non-degenera=
                                        cy condition [3, 10]. During this process, the
                                        Hessian of the objective
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">function is computed once, and the=
                                        gradient is computed </span>
                                    <!-- l. 30 -->
                                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"491" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                </m= jx-mi>
                                                <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop MathClass-open">
                                                        <mjx-c= class=3D"mjx-c28 TEX-S1">
                                                            </mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-mstyle class=3D"t=ext">
                                                            <mjx-mtext class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6C"></mjx-c>
                                                                <mjx-c cla=ss=3D"mjx-c6E"></mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mtext>
                                                        </mjx-mstyle>
                                                        <mjx-mfrac>
                                                            <mjx-frac>
                                                                <mjx-n= um>
                                                                    <mjx-nstrut></mjx-nstrut>
                                                                    <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                                            <m= jx-c class=3D"mjx-c31">
                                                                                </mjx-c>
                                                                        </mjx-mn>
                                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                                                    </mjx-num>
                                                                    <mjx-dbox>
                                                                        <mjx= -dtable>
                                                                            <mjx-line></mjx-line>
                                                                            <mjx-row>
                                                                                <mjx-den>
                                                                                    <mjx-dstrut></mjx-dstrut>
                                                                                    <mj= x-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716
                                                                                                TEX-I"=></mjx-c>
                                                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                                    </mjx-den>
                                                    </mjx-row>
                                                    </mjx-dtable>
                                                    </mjx-dbox>
                            </= mjx-frac>
                            </mjx-mfrac>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop MathClass-close">
                                <= mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29 TEX-S1">
                                    </mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-= assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                    xmlns=3D"http://=www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                    <mi>O</mi>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mo class==3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mstyle class=3D"text">
                                                <mtext>ln</mtext>
                                                </m= style>
                                                <mfrac>
                                                    <mrow>
                                                        <mn>1</mn>
                                                    </mrow>
                                                    <mrow>
                                                        <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                                                    </mrow>
                                                    </mfr= ac>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                    </mrow>
                                </math></mjx-assistive= -mml>
                                </mjx-container>
                                <span class=3D"cmr-9">times, where </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                <mjx-co= ntainer class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                                    tabindex=3D"0"=ctxtmenu_counter=3D"492" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position:
                                    relative;">
                                    <= mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                            <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I">
                                                </mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                        </mjx-math>
                                        <mjx-assistive-m= ml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/=1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                                <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                                            </math></mjx-assi= stive-mml>
                                            </mjx-container>
                                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the desired accuracy of the solution f=
                                                or our problem.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nguyen2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-341" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Nguyen, Duc Manh. =E2=80=9CA Combination of =
            CMAES-APOP Algorithm and Quasi-Newton
            Method=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">Advanced Computati=
                onal Methods for Knowledge Engineering</span>. Ed. by
            Hoai An Le
            Thi et al. Cham: Springer International Publishing, 2020, pp. 6=
            4=E2=80=9374. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">isbn</spa= n></span>: 978-3-030-38364-0.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper, we present an
                approach for combining the CMAES-APOP with a local search
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">in order to make a hybrid evolutio=
                    nary algorithm. This combination is based on
                    the information of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">population size in the evolution p=
                    rocess of the CMAES-APOP algorithm while the
                    local search is
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">quasi-Newton line search algorithm=
                    . We will give some conditions to efficiently
                    active the local search
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">inside CMAES-APOP. Some numerical =
                    experiments on multi-modal optimization
                    problems will show
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the efficiency of proposed approac=
                    h.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nie2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-342" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Nie, Bin, Adwait Jog, and Evgenia Smirni. =
            =E2=80=9CCharacterizing Accuracy-Aware Resilience of
            GPGPU Applications=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">Procee=
                dings of the 20th IEEE/ACM International Symposium on
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">Cluster, Cloud and Internet Comp=
                uting</span>. CCGRID 2020. 2020, pp. 111=E2=80=93120.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Graphics Processing Units (GPUs)
                have rapidly evolved to enable energy-efficient
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">data-parallel computing. In additi=
                    on to achieving exascale performance at a
                    stringent power budget,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">it is imperative for GPUs to provi=
                    de reliable computing guarantees to the end
                    user. In current
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">commodity systems, such guarantees=
                    are often achieved by incurring high
                    protection cost in terms
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of performance, power, and hardwar=
                    e resources. However, we argue that these
                    strict guarantees
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">are often not required (and that t=
                    he associated protected overheads can be
                    significantly reduced)
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">because several GPGPU applications=
                    are either fault-tolerant or can accept a
                    quantifiable loss in
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">output quality. To this end, this =
                    paper characterizes in a hierarchical manner
                    the accuracy-aware
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">resilience of GPGPU applications c=
                    onsisting of thousands of threads. This
                    characterization study shows
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">that accuracy-aware error resilien=
                    ce exhibits several interesting patterns across
                    threads at different
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">hierarchies (i.e., kernel/thread-b=
                    lock/warp). The insights from this
                    characterization study can be used
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">to reduce the overheads of expensi=
                    ve protection or recovery mechanisms that are
                    typically used by
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">GPUs to ensure application reliabi=
                    lity.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Nolet2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-343" class=3D"thebibliography">

        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Nolet, Corey J. et al. =E2=80=9CBringing UMA=
            P Closer to the Speed of Light with GPU Acceleration=E2=80=9D.
            In: (Aug. 2020). arXiv: 2008.00325v1 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[c=
                s.LG]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">The Uniform Manifold
                Approximation and Projection (UMAP) algorithm has become wid=
                ely
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">popular for its ease of use, quali=
                    ty of results, and support for exploratory,
                    unsupervised, supervised,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and semi-supervised learning. Whil=
                    e many algorithms can be ported to a GPU in a
                    simple and
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">direct fashion, such efforts have =
                    resulted in inefficent and inaccurate versions
                    of UMAP. We show a
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">number of techniques that can be u=
                    sed to make a faster and more faithful GPU
                    version of UMAP,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and obtain speedups of up to 100x =
                    in practice. Many of these design
                    choices/lessons are general
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">purpose and may inform the convers=
                    ion of other graph and manifold learning
                    algorithms to use GPUs.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Our implementation has been made p=
                    ublicly available as part of the open source
                    RAPIDS cuML
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">library(https://github.com/rapidsa=
                    i/cuml).</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Ozkaya2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-344" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Ozkaya, M. Yusuf et al. =E2=80=9CA scalable =
            graph generation algorithm to sample over a
            given shell distribution=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">=
                Proceedings of the 2020 IEEE International Parallel and
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">Distributed Processing Symposium=
                Workshops</span>. IPDPSW =E2=80=9920. IEEE, May 2020.
            <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">doi</span>
                <= /span>:
                    10.1109/ipdpsw50202.2020.00051.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Graphs are commonly used to model
                the relationships between various entities. These graphs
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">can be enormously large and thus, =
                    scalable graph analysis has been the subject of
                    many research efforts.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">To enable scalable analytics, many=
                    researchers have focused on generating
                    realistic graphs that support
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">controlled experiments for underst=
                    anding how algorithms perform under changing
                    graph features.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Significant progress has been made=
                    on scalable graph generation which preserve
                    some important graph
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">properties (e.g., degree distribut=
                    ion, clustering coefficients). In this paper,
                    we study how to sample
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">a graph from the space of graphs w=
                    ith a given shell distribution. Shell
                    distribution is related to the</span>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"493" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </m= jx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">=
                        <math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>k=
                            </mi>
                        </math>
                    </mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">-core=
                    ,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">which is the largest subgraph wher=
                    e each vertex is connected to at least </span>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"494" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </m= jx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">=
                        <math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>k=
                            </mi>
                        </math>
                    </mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">other
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">vertices. A </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <= mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex==3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"495" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relat=ive;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assi= stive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.=w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>k</mi>
                        </math></mjx-assistiv= e-mml>
                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">-shell
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">is the subset of vertices that are=
                            in </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_=0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"496" style=3D"font-size=: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"t=rue">
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mj= x-mi>
                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">
                                <= math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                    <mi>k<= /mi></math>
                            </mjx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">-core
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">but not </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-= container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                            tabindex=3D"0=" ctxtmenu_counter=3D" 497" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;"=>
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mo class==3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mo>
                                    <mjx-mr= ow>
                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-= mi>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2=B"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx=-c31"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mn>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                            <= mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29">
                                </mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assisti= ve-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.=org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mrow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-open"=>(</mo>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mi>k</mi>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">+</mo>
                                        <mn>1</mn>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <= mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                </mrow>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-= container><span class=3D"cmr-9">-core,
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and the shell distribution compris=
                                es the sizes of these shells. Core
                                decompositions are widely used to
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">extract information from graphs an=
                                d to assist other computations. We present a
                                scalable shared and
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">distributed memory graph generator=
                                that, given a shell decomposition, generates a
                                random graph that
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">conforms to it. Our extensive expe=
                                rimental results show the efficiency and
                                scalability of our methods.
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Our algorithm generates </span>
                            <!-= - l. 30 -->
                                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTM=L" tabindex=3D"0"
                                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"498" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; posi=tion: relative;">
                                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                        <mjx-mn c=lass=3D"mjx-n">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c33">
                </= mjx-c>
                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c33"></mjx-c>
                </mjx-mn>
                </mjx-math>
                <mjx-assistive-m= ml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                        xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/=1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                        <mn>233</mn>
                    </math></mjx-assistive-mml=>
                    </mjx-container>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">vertices and </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-co= ntainer class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                        tabindex=3D"0"=ctxtmenu_counter=3D"499" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                        <= mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c32">
                                    </mjx-c>
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c33"></mjx-c>
                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c37"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mn>
                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D="on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                    xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"=display=3D"inline">
                                    <mn>237</mn>
                                </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-container>
                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">edges in less than 50 seconds on 384 core=
                                s.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Pachajoa2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-345" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Pachajoa, Carlos et al. =E2=80=9CAlgorithm-B=
            ased Checkpoint-Recovery for the Conjugate Gradient
            Method=E2=80=9D. In: (July 2020). arXiv: 2007.04066v1 <span cla=ss=3D"cmtt-10">[cs.DC]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">As computers reach exascale and
                beyond, the incidence of faults will increase. Solutions to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">this problem are an active researc=
                    h topic. We focus on strategies to make the
                    preconditioned conjugate
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">gradient (PCG) solver resilient ag=
                    ainst node failures, specifically, the exact
                    state reconstruction (ESR)
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">method, which exploits redundancie=
                    s in PCG. Reducing the frequency at which
                    redundant information
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">is stored lessens the runtime over=
                    head. However, after the node failure, the
                    solver must restart from
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the last iteration for which redun=
                    dant information was stored, which increases
                    recovery overhead.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">This formulation highlights the me=
                    thod=E2=80=99s similarities to checkpoint-restart (CR).
                    Thus, this method,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">which we call ESR with periodic st=
                    orage (ESRP), can be considered a form of
                    algorithm-based
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">checkpoint-restart. The state is s=
                    tored implicitly, by exploiting redundancy
                    inherent to the algorithm,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">rather than explicitly as in CR. W=
                    e also minimize the amount of data to be stored
                    and retrieved
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">compared to CR, but additional com=
                    putation is required to reconstruct the
                    solver=E2=80=99s state. In this paper,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">we describe the necessary modifica=
                    tions to ESR to convert it into ESRP, and
                    perform an experimental
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">evaluation. We compare ESRP experi=
                    mentally with previously-existing ESR and
                    application-level
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">in-memory CR. Our results confirm =
                    that the overhead for ESR is reduced
                    significantly, both in the

                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">failure-free case, and if node fai=
                    lures are introduced. In the former case, the
                    overhead of ESRP is
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">usually lower than that of CR. How=
                    ever, CR is faster if node failures happen. We
                    claim that these
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">differences can be alleviated by t=
                    he implementation of more appropriate
                    preconditioners.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Page2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-346" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Page, Brian A. and Peter M. Kogge. =E2=80=9C=
            Scalability of Sparse Matrix Dense Vector Multiply
            (SpMV) on a Migrating Thread Architecture=E2=80=9D. In: <span c=lass=3D"cmti-10">Proceedings of the 2020
                IEEE
                International
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">Parallel and Distributed Process=
                ing Symposium Workshops</span>. IPDPSW =E2=80=9920.
            IEEE, May 2020.
            <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">doi</span>
                <= /span>: 10.1109/ipdpsw50202.2020.00088.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Sparse matrix dense vector
                multiplication (SpMV), exhibits the memory bandwidth and
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">communication driven nature of man=
                    y sparse linear algebra operations. Irregular
                    memory accesses from
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the non-zero structure within a sp=
                    arse matrix wreak havoc on performance. This
                    paper presents strong
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">scaling for communication avoiding=
                    SpMV implementations on a migrating thread
                    system intended to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">address the lack of locality in sp=
                    arse problems. We developed communication
                    avoiding SpMV code to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">attempt to reduce off-node thread =
                    migration by using the hypergraph partitioning
                    package HYPE to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">determine workload distribution. A=
                    dditionally, we investigate the performance
                    impact of overlapping
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">communication and computation thro=
                    ugh the use of remote memory operations
                    supported by the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">architecture. Incorporating remote=
                    memory operations with hypergraph partitioning
                    we achieved 6.18X
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">speedup for overall performance.</= span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Palleschi2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-347" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Palleschi, Alessandro et al. =E2=80=9CTime-O=
            ptimal Trajectory Planning for Flexible Joint Robots=E2=80=9D.
            In:
            <span class=3D"cmti-10">IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters </= span>(2020), pp. 1=E2=80=931. <span
                    class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small=-caps">doi</span></span>: 10.1109/lra.2020.2965861.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper, a new approach is
                proposed to optimally plan the motion along a parametrized
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">path for flexible joint robots, i.=
                    e., robots whose structure is purposefully
                    provided with compliant
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">elements. State-of-the-art methods=
                    efficiently solve the problem in case of
                    torque-controlled rigid
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">robots via a translation of the op=
                    timal control problem into a convex
                    optimization problem. Recently,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">we showed that, for jerk-controlle=
                    d rigid robots, the problem could be recast
                    into a non-convex
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">optimization problem. The non-conv=
                    exity is given by bilinear constraints that can
                    be efficiently handled
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">through McCormick relaxations and =
                    spatial Branch-and-Bound techniques. In this
                    paper, we show
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">that, even in case of robots with =
                    flexible joints, the time-optimal trajectory
                    planning problem can
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">be recast into a non-convex proble=
                    m in which the non-convexity is still given by
                    bilinear constraints.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">We performed experimental tests on=
                    a planar 2R elastic manipulator to validate
                    the benefits of the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">proposed approach. The scalability=
                    of the method for robots with multiple degrees
                    of freedom is also
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">discussed.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Panagiotas2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-348" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Panagiotas, Ioannis and Bora U=C3=A7ar. =E2=
            =80=9CEngineering fast almost optimal algorithms for
            bipartite
            graph matching=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">Proceeding=
                s of the 2020 European Symposium on Algorithms</span>.
            2020. HAL:
            02463717. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">u=
                    rl</span></span>: <a href=3D"https://hal.inria.fr/hal-02463717v3/document"=class=3D"url"><span
                    class=3D"cmtt-10">https://hal.inria.fr/hal-02463717v3/d=
                    ocument</span></a>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">We consider the maximum
                cardinality matching problem in bipartite graphs. There are =
                a
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">number of exact, deterministic alg=
                    orithms for this purpose, whose complexities
                    are high in practice.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">There are randomized approaches fo=
                    r special classes of bipartite graphs. Random
                    2-out bipartite
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">graphs, where each vertex chooses =
                    two neighbors at random from the other side,
                    form one class for which
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">there is an </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <= mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex==3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"500" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relat=ive;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2="><mjx-mo class=3D" mjx-n MathClass-open">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                            <= /mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45A TEX-I"=></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                        <mjx-c=class=3D"mjx-c2B"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mo>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"3">
                                        <mjx= -c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I">
                                            </mjx-c>
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D459 TEX-I">
                                                </m= jx-c>
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45C TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D454 T=EX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    </mjx-mrow=>
                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                        <= /mjx-mo>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" displa=y=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"=inline">
                            <mi>O</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>m</mi>=
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">+</mo>
                                    <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">nlogn</mi>
                                    </mr= ow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </= mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">-time
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Monte Carlo algorithm. Regular bip=
                    artite graphs, where all vertices have the same
                    degree, form another
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">class for which there is an expect=
                    ed </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0=" jax=3D" CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"501" style=3D"font-size:=117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"tr=ue">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx= -mi>
                            <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                    <mjx-c cla=ss=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c1D45A TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-=bin" space=3D"3">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2B"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mo>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D="mjx-i" space=3D"3">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c1D459 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45C TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c1D454 TEX-I">
                                            </mjx-c>
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I">
                                                </= mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                    <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml u=nselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998=/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>O</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-ope=n">(</mo>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>m</mi>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">+</mo>
                                    <mi mathvariant==3D"italic">nlogn</mi>
                                </mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                            </mrow>
                            </m= ath>
                    </mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">-time
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Las Vegas algorithm. We investigat=
                    e these two algorithms and turn them into
                    practical heuristics
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">with randomization. Experimental r=
                    esults show that the heuristics are fast and
                    obtain near optimal
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">matchings. They are also more robu=
                    st than the state of the art heuristics used in
                    the cardinality
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">matching algorithms, and are gener=
                    ally more useful as initialization
                    routines.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Parger2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-349" class=3D"thebibliography">

        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Parger, Mathias et al. =E2=80=9CspECK: accel=
            erating GPU sparse matrix-matrix multiplication through
            lightweight analysis=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">Proc=
                eedings of the 25th ACM SIGPLAN Symposium on Principles
                and
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">Practice of Parallel Programming=
            </span>. PPoPP =E2=80=9920. ACM, Feb. 2020. <span
                class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span=class=3D"small-caps">doi</span></span>: 10.1145/3332466.3374521.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">parse general matrix-matrix
                multiplication on GPUs is challenging due to the varying spa=
                rsity
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">patterns of sparse matrices. Exist=
                    ing solutions achieve good performance for
                    certain types of matrices,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">but fail to accelerate all kinds o=
                    f matrices in the same manner. Our approach
                    combines multiple
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">strategies with dynamic parameter =
                    selection to dynamically choose and tune the
                    best fitting algorithm
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">for each row of the matrix. This c=
                    hoice is supported by a lightweight,
                    multi-level matrix analysis,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">which carefully balances analysis =
                    cost and expected performance gains. Our
                    evaluation on thousands
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of matrices with various character=
                    istics shows that we outperform all currently
                    available solutions in
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">79% over all matrices with =C2=BF1=
                    5k products and that we achieve the second best
                    performance in 15%.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">For these matrices, our solution i=
                    s on average 83% faster than the second best
                    approach and up to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">25</span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-contai= ner class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxt=menu_counter=3D"502" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-= math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClas=s-bin">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-cD7"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assistive-= mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org=/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=C3=97</m= o>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-container>
                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">faster than other state-of-the-art GPU im=
                                plementations. Using our approach, applications
                                can expect
                            </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">great performance independent of t=
                                he matrices they work on.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Perez2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-350" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Perez, Alberto Cabrera et al. =E2=80=9CA dyn=
            amic Multi-Objective approach for dynamic load
            balancing
            in heterogeneous systems=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">=
                IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
            </span>(2020),
            pp. 1=E2=80=931. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-=caps">doi</span></span>:
            10.1109/tpds.2020.2989869.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Modern standards in High
                Performance Computing (HPC) have started to consider energy
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">consumption and power draw as a li=
                    miting factor. New and more complex
                    architectures have been
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">introduced in HPC systems to affor=
                    d these new restrictions, and include
                    coprocessors such as GPGPUs
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">for intensive computational tasks.=
                    As systems increase in heterogeneity, workload
                    distribution becomes
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">a more core problem to achieve the=
                    maximum efficiency in every computational
                    component. We
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">present a Multi-Objective Dynamic =
                    Load Balancing (DLB) approach where several
                    objectives can be
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">applied to tune an application. Th=
                    ese objectives can be dynamically exchanged
                    during the execution
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of an algorithm to better adapt to=
                    the resources available in a system. We have
                    implemented
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the Multi-Objective DLB together w=
                    ith a generic heuristic engine, designed to
                    perform multiple
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">strategies for DLB in iterative pr=
                    oblems. We also present Ull Multiobjective
                    Framework (UllMF),
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">an open-source tool that implement=
                    s the Multi-Objective generic approach. UllMF
                    separates metric
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">gathering, objective functions to =
                    be optimized and load balancing algorithms, and
                    improves code
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">portability using a simple interfa=
                    ce to reduce the costs of new implementations.
                    We illustrate how
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">performance and energy consumption=
                    are improved for the implemented techniques,
