                        </math></mjx-assistive= -mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">and </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container c=lass=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_=counter=3D"518" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math=class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mfenced>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-lo=p">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c5B TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mi class==3D"mjx-n qopname">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c65"></mjx-c>
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c78="></mjx-c><mjx-c class=3D" mjx-c70"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mi>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n"=space=3D"2">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2061"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mfenced>
                                    <mjx-= mo class=3D"mjx-lop">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c7B TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        <mjx-= mrow>
                                            <mjx-mfrac>
                                                <mjx-frac>
                                                    <mjx-num>
                                                        <mjx-nstrut></mjx-nstrut>
                                                        <mjx-mrow size==3D"s">
                                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                </= mjx-mi>
                                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                                    </mjx-num>
                                                    <mjx-dbox>
                                                        <mjx-dtable>
                                                            <mjx-line></mjx-line>
                                                            <mjx= -row>
                                                                <mjx-den>
                                                                    <mjx-dstrut></mjx-dstrut>
                                                                    <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                        <mjx-mi class==3D"mjx-i">
                                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                                                    </= mjx-den>
                                                                    </mjx-row>
                                                        </mjx-dtable>
                                                    </mjx-dbox>
                                                </mjx-frac>
                                            </mjx-mfrac>
                                            <mjx-mi cl=ass=3D"mjx-n qopname">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6C"></mjx-c>
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-=c6E"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"m=jx-c2061"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            <mjx-mfrac>
                                                <mjx-frac>
                                                    <mjx-num>
                                                        <mjx-nstrut>
                                        </mjx-= nstrut>
                                        <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D=43F TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                        </mjx-num>
                                        <mjx-dbox>
                                            <mjx-dtable>
                                                <mjx= -line>
                                                    </mjx-line>
                                                    <mjx-row>
                                                        <mjx-den>
                                                            <mjx-dstrut></mjx-dstrut>
                                                            <mjx-mrow size==3D"s">
                                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D707 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                    </= mjx-mi>
                                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                                        </mjx-den>
                                                    </mjx-row>
                                            </mjx-dtable>
                                        </mjx-dbox>
                                        </mjx-frac>
                                        </= mjx-mfrac>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-lop">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c7D TEX-=S2"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mfenced>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" sp=ace=3D"3">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-=n" space=3D"3">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mn>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-mo= class=3D"mjx-lop">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c5D TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            </mjx-m= fenced>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                </= mjx-mi>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-lop MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"=mjx-c2F TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                    <mjx-c= class=3D"mjx-c32">
                                        </mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mn>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselecta=ble=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/Ma=thML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mfenced open=3D"[" separators=3D"" close=3D"]">
                                <m= row>
                                    <mi class=3D"qopname">exp</mi>
                                    <mo>=E2=81=A1</mo>
                                    <mfenced open=3D"{" sep=arators=3D"" close=3D"}">
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mfrac>
                                                <mrow>
                                                    <mi>n</mi>
                                                </mrow>
                                                <mrow>
                                                    <mi>k
                                </m= i>
                                </mrow>
                                </mfrac>
                                <mi class=3D"qopname">ln</mi>
                                <mo>=E2=81=A1</mo>
                                <mfrac>
                                    <mro= w>
                                        <mi>L</mi>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mi>=CE=BC</mi>
                                        </mrow>
                                </mfrac>
                                </mrow>
                            </mfenced>
                            <mo= class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                <mn>1</mn>
                                </mrow>
                                </mfenced>
                                <mi>k</mi=>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=95</mo>
                                    <mn>2</mn>
                        </math></mjx-assistive= -mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">respectively, where </span><!-- l. 30 -->=
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex==3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"519" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relat=ive;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assi= stive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.=w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>k</mi>
                        </math></mjx-assistiv= e-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the iteration counter, </span>
                <!-- l. =
30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" ta=bindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"520" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position:=relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class==3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <m= jx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http=://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>n</mi>
                        </math></mjx-a= ssistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the dimension of the problem, </span>
                <= !-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CH=TML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"521" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; po=sition: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mi= class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D707 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-m= ath>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        xmlns==3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>=CE=BC</mi>
                                        <= /math>
                                </mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-container>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the strong convexity parameter, and
                </= span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax==3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"522" style=3D"font-size: 117.=4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <= mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D43F TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <= /mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        x=mlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>L</mi>
                            </= math>
                            </mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the Lipschitz constant of the gradient=
                    . Currently, these are the best known
                    superlinear convergence
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">rates for these methods. In partic=
                    ular, our results show that the starting moment
                    of superlinear
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">convergence of BFGS method depends=
                    on the logarithm of the condition number
                </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_A=ttached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"523" style=3D"f=ont-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hid=den=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mfrac>
                            <mjx-frac>
                                <mjx-num>
                                    <mjx-nstrut></mjx-nstrut>
                                    <mjx-mr= ow size=3D"s">
                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D43F TEX-I">
                                                </m= jx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                </mjx-num>
                                <mjx-dbox>
                                    <mjx-dtable>
                                        <mjx-line>
                                            </mjx-li= ne>
                                            <mjx-row>
                                                <mjx-den>
                                                    <mjx-dstrut></mjx-dstrut>
                                                    <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                        <mjx-mi=class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D707 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                        </mjx-mr= ow>
                                                </mjx-den>
                                            </mjx-row>
                                    </mjx-dtable>
                                </mjx-dbox>
                            </mjx-frac>
                        </mjx-mfrac>
                        </mjx= -math>
                        <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns==3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mfrac>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mi=>L</mi>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mi>=CE=BC</mi>
                                    </mrow>
                                </mfrac>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mm= l>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">in the worst case.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Rodomanov2020a" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-366" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">=E2=80=94 =E2=80=9CRates of Superlinear Conv=
            ergence for Classical Quasi-Newton Methods=E2=80=9D. In: (Mar. 2020).
            arXiv: 2003.09174v1 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[math.OC]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">We study the local convergence of
                classical quasi-Newton methods for nonlinear optimization.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Although it was well established a=
                    long time ago that asymptotically these
                    methods converge superlinearly,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the corresponding rates of converg=
                    ence still remain unknown. In this paper, we
                    address this problem.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">We obtain first explicit non-asymp=
                    totic rates of superlinear convergence for the
                    standard quasi-Newton
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">methods, which are based on the up=
                    dating formulas from the convex Broyden class.
                    In particular, for
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the well-known DFP and BFGS method=
                    s, we obtain the rates of the form </span>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"524" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                        <mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-lop MathClass-open">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mj=x-c28 TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mfrac>
                                    <mjx-frac>
                                        <mjx-num>
                                            <mjx= -nstrut>
                                                </mjx-nstrut>
                                                <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                        <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D43F TEX-I"></mjx-c>=
                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                                    <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mn>=
                                                </mjx-mrow>
                                        </mjx-num>
                                        <mjx-dbox>
                                            <mjx-dtable>
                                                <mjx-line></mjx-line>
                                                <mjx-row>
                                                    <m= jx-den>
                                                        <mjx-dstrut></mjx-dstrut>
                                                        <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i="><mjx-c class=3D" mjx-c1D707 TEX-I">
                                                                </mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mi>
                                                            <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n="><mjx-c class=3D" mjx-c32">
                                                                </mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mn>
                                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                <mjx-c=class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mi>
                                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                                        </mjx-den>
                                                </mjx-row>=
                                            </mjx-dtable>
                                        </mjx-dbox>
                                    </mjx-frac>
                                </mjx-mfrac>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"=mjx-lop MathClass-close">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29 TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <= /mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I">
                                        </mjx-c=>
                                </mjx-mi>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-lop MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c2F TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                    <m= jx-c class=3D"mjx-c32">
                                        </mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mn>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unsel=ectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Mat=h/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                <mrow=>
                                    <mfrac>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">nL</mi>
                                            <mn>2</mn>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mi>=
                                                =CE=BC</mi>
                                            <mn>2</mn>
                                            <mi>k</mi>
                                        </mrow>
                                    </mfrac>
                            </mrow>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass=-close">)</mo>
                            </mrow>
                            <mi>k</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=95</mo>
                            <m= n>2</mn>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">and </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container c=lass=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_=counter=3D"525" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math=class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-lop M=athClass-open">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28 TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mrow>
                                <= mjx-mfrac>
                                    <mjx-frac>
                                        <mjx-num>
                                            <mjx-nstrut></mjx-nstrut>
                                            <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">=
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c1D43F TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mi>
                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                        </mjx-num>
                                        <mjx-dbox>
                                            <m= jx-dtable>
                                                <mjx-line></mjx-line>
                                                <mjx-row>
                                                    <mjx-den>
                                                        <mjx-dstrut></mjx-dstrut>
                                                        <= mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D707 TEX-=I"></mjx-c>
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mi>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            </= mjx-den>
                            </mjx-row>
                            </mjx-dtable>
                            </mjx-dbox>
                            </mjx-frac>
                            </mjx-mfrac>
                            </mjx-mrow=>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-lop MathClass-close">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29 TEX-S2">=
                                </mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D=458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-lop MathClass-bin" space==3D"3">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2F TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mj=x-n" space=3D"3">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mn>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-= assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://=www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClas=s-open">(</mo>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mfrac>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">nL</mi>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <= mrow>
                                            <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">=CE=BCk</mi>
                                </mrow>
                                </mfrac>
                            </mrow>
                            <mo class==3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                            </mrow>
                            <mi>k</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=
                                =88=95</mo>
                            <mn>2</mn>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">respectively, where </span><!-- l. 30 -->=
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex==3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"526" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relat=ive;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D458 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assi= stive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.=w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>k</mi>
                        </math></mjx-assistiv= e-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the iteration counter, </span>
                <!-- l. =
30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" ta=bindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"527" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position:=relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class==3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45B TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <m= jx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http=://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>n</mi>
                        </math></mjx-a= ssistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the dimension of the problem, </span>
                <= !-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CH=TML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"528" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; po=sition: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mi= class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D707 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-m= ath>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        xmlns==3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>=CE=BC</mi>
                                        <= /math>
                                </mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-container>

                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the strong convexity parameter, and
                </= span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax==3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"529" style=3D"font-size: 117.=4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <= mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D43F TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <= /mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        x=mlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>L</mi>
                            </= math>
                            </mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the Lipschitz constant of the gradient=
                    .</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Roy2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-367" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Roy, Abhishek et al. =E2=80=9CEscaping Saddl=
            e-Points Faster under Interpolation-like Conditions=E2=80=9D.
            In:
            (Sept. 2020). arXiv: 2009.13016 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[stat.M=
                L]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper, we show that under
                over-parametrization several standard stochastic optimizatio=
                n
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">algorithms escape saddle-points an=
                    d converge to local-minimizers much faster. One
                    of the fundamental
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">aspects of over-parametrized model=
                    s is that they are capable of interpolating the
                    training data. We show
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">that, under interpolation-like ass=
                    umptions satisfied by the stochastic gradients
                    in an over-parametrization
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">setting, the first-order oracle co=
                    mplexity of Perturbed Stochastic Gradient
                    Descent (PSGD) algorithm
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">to reach an </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <= mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex==3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"530" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relat=ive;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assi= stive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.=w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                        </math></m= jx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">-local-minimizer,
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">matches the corresponding determin=
                            istic rate of </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu=_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"531" style=3D="font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-h=idden=3D"true">
                                <mjx-mover>
                                    <mjx-over style=3D"padding-bottom: 0.105em; paddi=ng-left: 0.586em; margin-bottom:
                                        -0.597em;">
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop" style==3D"width: 0px; margin-left: -0.278em;">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2DC TEX-S1">
                </= mjx-c>
                </mjx-mo>
                </mjx-over>
                <mjx-base>
                    <mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-sc mjx-b=mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4AA TEX-SC"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-mrow>
                    </mj= x-base>
                    </mjx-mover>
                    <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-o=pen">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mn class=3D"m=jx-n">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mn>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n Math=Class-bin" space=3D"3">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2F"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mi cl=ass=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"3">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-m= i>
                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mn>
                                </mjx-m= row>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29">
                                        </mjx-= c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" dis=play=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display==3D"inline">
                                <mover accent=3D"true">
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mi mathvariant=3D"bold-script">=
                                            =F0=9D=92=AA</mi>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mo accent=3D"true">=CC=83</mo>
                                </mover>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mo cl=ass=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mn>1</mn>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=
                                            =E2=88=95</mo>
                                        <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                                        <mn>2</mn>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-=close">)</mo>
                                </mrow>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container><span class==3D"cmr-9">.
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">We next analyze Stochastic Cubic-R=
                            egularized Newton (SCRN) algorithm under
                            interpolation-like
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">conditions, and show that the orac=
                            le complexity to reach an </span>
                        <!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"532" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </m= jx-mi>
                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">=
                                <math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                    <mi>=
                                        =F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                                </math>
                            </mjx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"=cmr-9">-local-minimizer
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">under interpolation-like condition=
                            s, is </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attache=d_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"533" style=3D"font-si=ze: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D="true">
                                <mjx-mover>
                                    <mjx-over style=3D"padding-bottom: 0.105em; padding-left:=0.586em; margin-bottom:
                                        -0.597em;">
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop" style=3D"widt=h: 0px; margin-left: -0.278em;">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2DC TEX-S1"></mjx-c>
                                            </= mjx-mo>
                                    </mjx-over>
                                    <mjx-base>
                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-sc mjx-b mjx-i">=
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4AA TEX-SC"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                    </mjx-base>
                                    <= /mjx-mover>
                                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                                <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c28">
                                                    </mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            <mjx-mrow>
                                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                    <m= jx-c class=3D"mjx-c31">
                                                        </mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mn>
                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bi=n" space=3D"3">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2F"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"m=jx-i" space=3D"3">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                <mjx-m= n class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mn>
                                                    <mjx-mo class==3D"mjx-n MathClass-punc">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2E"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mn= class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c35"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mn>
                                                        </mj= x-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29">
                                                                </m= jx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                            </mjx-mrow>
                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on"=display=3D"inline"><math
                                    xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" displ=ay=3D"inline">
                                    <mover accent=3D"true">
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mi mathvariant=3D"bold-script">=
                                                =F0=9D=92=AA</mi>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mo accent=3D"true">=CC=83</mo>
                                    </mover>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mo cl=ass=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mn>1</mn>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=
                                                =E2=88=95</mo>
                                            <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                                            <mn>2</mn>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-punc">.=
                                            </mo>
                                            <mn>5</mn>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                    </mrow>
                                </math></m= jx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">.
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">While this obtained complexity is =
                            better than the corresponding complexity of
                            either PSGD, or SCRN
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">without interpolation-like assumpt=
                            ions, it does not match the rate of </span>
                        <!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"534" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                                <mjx-mover>
                                    <mjx-over style=3D"padding-bottom: 0.105em; padding-left:=0.586em; margin-bottom:
                                        -0.597em;">
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop" style=3D"width=: 0px; margin-left: -0.278em;">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2DC TEX-S1"></mjx-c>
                                            </m= jx-mo>
                                    </mjx-over>
                                    <mjx-base>
                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-sc mjx-b mjx-i">
                                                <= mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4AA TEX-SC">
                                                    </mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                    </mjx-base>
                                    </= mjx-mover>
                                    <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                            <mjx= -c class=3D"mjx-c28">
                                                </mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c31">
                                                    </mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mn>
                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin=" space=3D" 3">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2F"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mj=x-i" space=3D"3">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                            <mjx-mn= class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mn>
                                                <mjx-mo class=3D="mjx-n MathClass-punc">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2E"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mo>
                                                <mjx-mn cl=ass=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c35"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mn>
                                                </mjx-m= row>
                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29">
                                                        </mjx-= c>
                                                </mjx-mo>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" dis=play=3D"inline"><math
                                    xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display==3D"inline">
                                    <mover accent=3D"true">
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mi mathvariant=3D"bold-script">=
                                                =F0=9D=92=AA</mi>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mo accent=3D"true">=CC=83</mo>
                                    </mover>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mo cl=ass=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mn>1</mn>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=
                                                =E2=88=95</mo>
                                            <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                                            <mn>1</mn>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-punc">.=
                                            </mo>
                                            <mn>5</mn>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                    </mrow>
                                </math></m= jx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container>
                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">corresponding to deterministic Cubic-Regu=
                            larized Newton method. It seems further
                            Hessian-based
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">interpolation-like assumptions are=
                            necessary to bridge this gap. We also discuss
                            the corresponding
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">improved complexities in the zerot=
                            h-order settings.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Said2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-368" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Said, Noureddine Ait, Mounir Benabdenbi, and=
            Katell Morin-Allory. =E2=80=9CFPU Bit-Width
            Optimization for Approximate Computing: A Non-Intrusive Approac=
            h=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">2020 15th
                Design
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">&amp; Technology of Integrated S=
                ystems in Nanoscale Era (DTIS)</span>. IEEE,
            Apr. 2020. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">=
                    doi</span></span>:
            10.1109/dtis48698.2020.9080931.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">F1oating-Point Units (FPUs) count
                as a significant part of computing resources in modern
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">general-purpose and application-sp=
                    ecific processors. Full-precision FPUs can be a
                    source of extensive
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">hardware overhead (power consumpti=
                    on, area, memory footprint etc.). On the other
                    hand, several
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">applications feature the inherent =
                    ability to tolerate precision loss. This has
                    lead to the development of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">a new computing paradigm: Transpre=
                    cision Computing (TC), where variable and
                    arbitrary precision
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">hardware FPUs have been introduced=
                    . Many tools and libraries have been proposed
                    to simulate
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the effects of approximation on ap=
                    plications, to help designers to select the
                    most optimized FPU
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">architecture adequate for a given =
                    application.However, existing techniques
                    require developers to rewrite
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">part or all of their existing soft=
                    ware stacks (applications, libraries, operating
                    systems =E2=80=A6), which is
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">often infeasible, complex or at le=
                    ast a very time-consuming development
                    effort.This work proposes a
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">non-intrusive approach, which does=
                    not need source code modification, by
                    introducing approximations
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">at the low-level in assembly. This=
                    allows approximating virtually all kinds of
                    executable binaries
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">(bare-metal applications, single- =
                    /multi-threaded user applications, OS/RTOS,
                    etc.).We implement the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">approach on top of the well known =
                    QEMU dynamic binary translator. We perform
                    experiments on a set
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of benchmarks from the literature,=
                    and we demonstrate how the approach further
                    simplifies evaluating
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the impact of FP approximations on=
                    numerical applications outputs, without being
                    intrusive to the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">source code.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Santen2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-369" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Santen, Victor M. van et al. =E2=80=9CMassiv=
            ely Parallel Circuit Setup in GPU-SPICE=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-1=0">IEEE
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">Transactions on Computers </span=>(2020). <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span
                        class=3D"small-caps">doi</span></sp= an>: 10.1109/tc.2020.3032343.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">SPICE simulations are the
                industry standard to analyze circuits for decades. However, =
                they
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">are computationally complex as eac=
                    h circuit is simulated at the transistor-level.
                    However, this is in
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">a direct conflict with the ever-in=
                    creasing demands of circuit designers in which
                    SPICE simulations
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">for large circuits (e.g., DSPs, AE=
                    S, etc.) at full accuracy are inevitably
                    required to fulfill new
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">industrial standards like automoti=
                    ve safety ISO 26262 with tool confidence level
                    1. To accelerate
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">SPICE simulation without sacrifici=
                    ng accuracy, state-of-the-art approaches have
                    started to employ

                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">GPUs to parallelize the LU-factori=
                    zation and device linearization phases. Instead
                    of focusing on these
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">phases, this work demonstrates for=
                    the first time that when large circuits come
                    into play, a new and
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">equally important performance bott=
                    leneck emerges at the circuit setup phase.
                    Speeding up the circuit
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">setup phase in SPICE is our key fo=
                    cus in this paper. Our two implementations
                    demonstrate that our
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">GPU-based circuit setup reduces th=
                    e analysis time from 4.5 days to merely 89
                    seconds for a 256-bit
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">multiplier, which consists of more=
                    than 1M transistors. Our achieved speedup is
                    4396x compared to the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">baseline (open-source NGSPICE) and=
                    more than 2x compared to commercial (HSPICE
                    and Spectre)
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">SPICE circuit setup.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Santos2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-370" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Santos, Fernando Fernandes dos et al. =E2=80=
            =9CReduced-Precision DWC for Mixed-Precision GPUs=E2=80=9D.
            In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">Proceedings of the 26th IEEE Intern=
                ational Symposium on On-Line Testing and Robust
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">System Design</span>. IOLTS 2020=
            . IEEE, July 2020. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">doi<= /span></span>:
                10.1109/iolts50870.2020.9159748.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Duplication with Comparison (DWC)
                is an effective software-level solution to improve
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the reliability of computing syste=
                    ms, including Graphics Processing Units (GPUs).
                    DWC, however,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">introduces performance and energy =
                    consumption overheads that could be
                    unacceptable for
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">High-Performance Computing (HPC) o=
                    r real-time safety-critical applications. In
                    this work, we propose
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Reduced-Precision DWC (RP-DWC): an=
                    improvement over the traditional DWC approach
                    that uses
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">mixed-precision GPUs hardware reso=
                    urces to implement fault detection. We
                    investigate, through both
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">fault injection campaigns and acce=
                    lerated neutron beam experiments, the impact of
                    RPDWC onto
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">performance, energy consumption, a=
                    nd its fault detection capabilites. We show
                    that RP-DWC achieves
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">on average 74% fault coverage (up =
                    to 86%) with very small overheads (0.1% time
                    and 24% energy
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">consumption overhead, in the best =
                    case).</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Sao2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-371" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Sao, Piyush et al. =E2=80=9CA supernodal all=
            -pairs shortest path algorithm=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">Procee=
                dings of the 25th
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">ACM SIGPLAN Symposium on Princip=
                les and Practice of Parallel
                Programming</span>. PPoPP =E2=80=9920.
            ACM, Feb. 2020. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-c=aps">doi</span></span>:
            10.1145/3332466.3374533.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">We show how to exploit graph
                sparsity in the Floyd-Warshall algorithm for the all-pairs
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">shortest path (Apsp) problem. Floy=
                    d-Warshall is an attractive choice for Apsp on
                    high-performing
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">systems due to its structural simi=
                    larity to solving dense linear systems and
                    matrix multiplication.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">However, if sparsity of the input =
                    graph is not properly exploited, Floyd-Warshall
                    will perform
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">unnecessary asymptotic work and th=
                    us may not be a suitable choice for many input
                    graphs. To overcome
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">this limitation, the key idea in o=
                    ur approach is to use the known algebraic
                    relationship between
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Floyd-Warshall and Gaussian elimin=
                    ation, and import several algorithmic
                    techniques from sparse
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Cholesky factorization, namely, fi=
                    ll-in reducing ordering, symbolic analysis,
                    supernodal traversal, and
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">elimination tree parallelism. When=
                    combined, these techniques reduce computation,
                    improve locality
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and enhance parallelism. We implem=
                    ent these ideas in an efficient shared memory
                    parallel prototype
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">that is orders of magnitude faster=
                    than an efficient multi-threaded baseline
                    Floyd-Warshall that does
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">not exploit sparsity. Our experime=
                    nts suggest that the Floyd-Warshall algorithm
                    can compete with
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Dijkstra=E2=80=99s algorithm (the =
                    algorithmic core of Johnson=E2=80=99s algorithm) for several
                    classes sparse graphs.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Sayegh2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-372" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Sayegh, Ammar T. Al and Elisa D. Sotelino. =
            =E2=80=9CA new row-wise parallel finite element analysis
            algorithm with dynamic load balancing=E2=80=9D. In: <span class==3D"cmti-10">International Journal of
                Earthquake and
                Impact
            </span><span class=3D"cmti-10">Engineering </span>3.2 (2020), p=
            . 120. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">doi</span></span=>: 10.1504/ijeie.2020.108588.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">A parallel scheme is devised to
                efficiently parallelise all steps of parallel finite element
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">analysis in this study. In additio=
                    n, this scheme is based on a row-wise matrix
                    distribution. A new
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">row-wise parallel finite element a=
                    nalysis algorithm that exploits the nature of
                    distributed compressed
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">row sparse matrices and multivecto=
                    rs to improve concurrency is developed. A new
                    dynamic load
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">balancing technique has also been =
                    devised. The dynamic load balancing technique
                    has been designed
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">specifically to balance the comput=
                    ational workload among processors suitable for
                    the analysis of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">nonlinear structures. This new alg=
                    orithm has been implemented in ParaStruc, which
                    is a parallel
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">structural analysis system. Trilin=
                    os, a set of parallel numerical libraries
                    developed by researchers in

                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the Sandia National Laboratory has=
                    been used to build this algorithm. ParaStruc
                    is a lightweight
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">fully parallelised parallel finite=
                    element analysis system, which contains only
                    three classes and a
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">pre-processor. It is shown that th=
                    is approach produces superior performance in
                    terms of speedup,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">efficiency, and isoefficiency in t=
                    he analysis of nonlinear structure response
                    ranges when compared to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">parallel ABAQUS. The performance a=
                    nd efficiency of this algorithm has been
                    verified with numerical
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">simulations of a 200-metre 50-stor=
                    y 10-frame 10-bay 3D structure subjected to
                    various load levels.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Schaefer2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-373" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Sch=C3=A4fer, Florian, Matthias Katzfuss, an=
            d Houman Owhadi. =E2=80=9CSparse Cholesky factorization by
            Kullback-Leibler minimization=E2=80=9D. In: (Apr. 2020). arXiv:=
            2004.14455v1 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[math.NA]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">We propose to compute a sparse
                approximate inverse Cholesky factor </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mj= x-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D="0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"535" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative=;">

                            <mi>L</mi>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-m= ml>
                        </mjx-container>
                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">of a dense covariance matrix </span>
                        <!-- =
l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"=tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"536" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; positi=on: relative;">

                            </mjx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container>
                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">by minimizing the Kullback-Leibler diverg=
                            ence between the Gaussian distributions </span>

                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"537" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">

                </mj= x-c>

                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D43F TEX-I"></mjx-c>

                <= mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">

                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable==3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathM=L" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi mathvariant=3D"bold-script">=F0=9D=92=A9</mi>

                                <mrow>
                                    <mn>0</mn>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass=-punc">,</mo>
                                    <mi>L</mi>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                    <mi class==3D"MathClass-op">=E2=8A=A4</mi>
                                    <mo>=E2=81=A1</mo>
                                    <mi>L</mi>
                                    <mo class=3D"Ma=thClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                    <mn>1</mn>
                                </mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)=
                                </mo>
                                </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9=">,
            </span><span class=3D" cmr-9">subject to a sparsity constraint. =
                        Surprisingly, this problem has a closed-form
                        solution that can be
                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">computed efficiently, recovering t=
                        he popular Vecchia approximation in spatial
                        statistics. Based on recent
                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">results on the approximate sparsit=
                        y of inverse Cholesky factors of </span>
                    <!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"539" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">

                            <math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mi>=
                                    =CE=98</mi>
                            </math>
                        </mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-container>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">obtained from pairwise evaluation of Gree=
                        n=E2=80=99s functions of elliptic boundary-value
                        problems at points</span>

                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" s=pace=3D"4">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2282"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i" space=3D"4">

                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">we propose an elimination ordering=
                            and sparsity pattern that allows us to compute

                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_A=ttached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"

ss=3D" cmr-9">-approximate
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">inverse Cholesky factors of such <= /span>

                                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">in computational complexity </span>
                                            <!-- l=
. 30 -->
                                            <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0"
                                                jax=3D"CHTML"=tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"543"
                                                style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; positio=n: relative;">

                                                    <mjx-mi clas=s=3D"mjx-sc mjx-b mjx-i">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4AA TEX-SC"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-m= i>
                                                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                                                <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mo>
                                                            <mjx-mrow>
                                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">

                                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">in space and </span><!-- l. 30 -->

                                                <mjx-mrow>

                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D441=TEX-I"></mjx-c>

                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-n qopname" space=3D"2">
                                                        <mjx-c= class=3D"mjx-c6C">

                                                    </mjx-mo>

                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                                            <mjx-= mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D441 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                                <mjx-= mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">

                                                                        </mjx= -c>

                                        </= mjx-math>
                                        <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                xm=lns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">

                                                    </mo>

                                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin"=>=E2=88=95</mo>
                                                        <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                                                    </mrow>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)<= /mo>
                                                </mrow>

                        </mjx-container>
                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">in time. To the best of our knowledge, th=
                            is is the best asymptotic complexity for this
                            class of problems.
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Furthermore, our method is embarra=
                            ssingly parallel, automatically exploits
                            low-dimensional structure
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">in the data, and can perform Gauss=
                            ian-process regression in linear (in </span>
                        <!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ctxtmenu_counter=3D"545" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D441 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </m= jx-mi>
                            </mjx-math>
                            <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">=
                                <math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                    <mi>N=
                                    </mi>
                                </math>
                            </mjx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">)
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">space complexity. Motivated by the=
                            optimality properties of our methods, we
                            propose methods for
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">applying it to the joint covarianc=
                            e of training and prediction points in
                            Gaussian-process regression,
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">greatly improving stability and co=
                            mputational cost. Finally, we show how to apply
                            our method to
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the important setting of Gaussian =
                            processes with additive noise, sacrificing
                            neither accuracy nor
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">computational complexity.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Schenker2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-374" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Schenker, Carla, Jeremy E. Cohen, and Evrim =
            Acar. =E2=80=9CA Flexible Optimization Framework
            for Regularized Matrix-Tensor Factorizations with Linear Coupli=
            ngs=E2=80=9D. In: (July 2020). arXiv:
            2007.09605v1 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[cs.LG]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Coupled matrix and tensor
                factorizations (CMTF) are frequently used to jointly analyze
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">data from multiple sources, also c=
                    alled data fusion. However, different
                    characteristics of datasets
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">stemming from multiple sources pos=
                    e many challenges in data fusion and require to
                    employ various
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">regularizations, constraints, loss=
                    functions and different types of coupling
                    structures between datasets.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper, we propose a flexib=
                    le algorithmic framework for coupled matrix and
                    tensor factorizations
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">which utilizes Alternating Optimiz=
