                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-=i">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D460 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45F TEX=-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                                </mjx-mrow>
                                                </mjx-num>
                                                <mjx-dbox>
                                                    <mjx-dtable>
                                                        <mjx-line>
                                                            <= /mjx-line>
                                                                <mjx-row>
                                                                    <mjx-den>
                                                                        <mjx-dstrut></mjx-dstrut>
                                                                        <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                            <= mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I">
                                                                                </mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <= mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mn>
                            </mjx-mrow=>
                            </mjx-den>
                            </mjx-row>
                            </mjx-dtable>
                            </mjx-dbox>
                            </mjx-frac>
                            </mjx-mfrac>
                            </mjx-m= row>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-sop MathClass-close">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29 TEX-S=1"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable==3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathM=L" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>O</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                <m= row>
                                    <mfrac>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">sr</mi>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mi>=F0=9D=
                                                =9C=96</mi>
                                            <mn>2</mn>
                                        </mrow>
                                    </mfrac>
                            </mrow>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)<= /mo>
                                    </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">and </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container c=lass=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_=counter=3D"559" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math=class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c1D442 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                            <mjx-mo class==3D"mjx-lop MathClass-open">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28 TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-m= o>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mfrac>
                                        <mjx-frac>
                                            <mjx-num>
                                                <mjx-nstrut></mjx-nstrut>
                                                <mjx-mro= w size=3D"s">
                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D460 TEX-I">
                                                            </mj= x-c>
                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                                    <mjx-msub>
                                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mj=x-c1D451 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mi>
                                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-script style=3D"vertical-a=lign: -0.15em;">
                                                            <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c1D706 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                                        </mjx-script>
                                                    </mjx-msub>
                                                    <= /mjx-mrow>
                                            </mjx-num>
                                            <mjx-dbox>
                                                <mjx-dtable>
                                                    <mjx-line></mjx-line>
                                                    <mjx-row>
                                                        <mj= x-den>
                                                            <mjx-dstrut></mjx-dstrut>
                                                            <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i"=>
                                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D716 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                                            </mjx-den>
                                                            </= mjx-row>
                                                </mjx-dtable>
                                            </mjx-dbox>
                                        </mjx-frac>
                                    </mjx-mfrac>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mo c=lass=3D"mjx-lop MathClass-close">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29 TEX-S2"></mjx-c>
                                    </= mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display==3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"i=nline">
                            <mi>O</mi>
                            <mrow>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mfrac>
                                        <mro= w>
                                            <mi>s</mi>
                                            <msub>
                                                <mrow>
                                                    <mi>d</mi>
                                                </mrow>
                                                <mrow>
                                                    <mi>=CE=BB</mi>
                                                </mrow>
                                                </msub=>
                                </mrow>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mi>=F0=9D=9C=96</mi>
                                </mrow>
                                </mfrac>
                            </mrow>
                            <mo class=3D"MathC=lass-close">)</mo>
                            </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">total sample complexity for matrix multip=
                    lication and ridge regression, respectively.
                    This matches
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the state-of-the-art bounds that a=
                    re obtained using global sketching matrices.
                    The localized nature of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">sketching considered allows for di=
                    fferent parts of the data matrix to be sketched
                    independently and
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">hence is more amenable to computat=
                    ion in distributed and streaming settings and
                    results in a smaller
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">memory and computational footprint=
                    .</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Steinerberger2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-393" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Steinerberger, Stefan. =E2=80=9CA Spectral A=
            pproach to the Shortest Path Problem=E2=80=9D. In: (Apr.&nbsp;2,
            2020).
            arXiv: 2004.01163v1 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[math.CO]</span>.

        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Let </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax=CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"560"=style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX=" aria-hidden=3D" true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D43A T=EX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                            <m= jx-c class=3D"mjx-c3D">
                                </mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"4">
                            <mjx-mo class==3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mr= ow>
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D449 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-= mi>
                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-punc">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2C"></mjx-c>=
                                    </mjx-mo>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"2">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D438 TEX=-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                            <mj= x-c class=3D"mjx-c29">
                                </mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive= -mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.or=g/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>G</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel=">=3D</mo><mrow><mo class=3D" MathClass-open">(</mo>
                            <mrow>
                                <mi>V</mi>
                                <mo clas=s=3D"MathClass-punc">,</mo>
                                <mi>E</mi>
                            </mrow>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)=
                            </mo>
                            </mrow>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">be a simple, connected graph. One is ofte=
                    n interested in a short path between two
                    vertices </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJa=x CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"561=" style=3D" font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-T=EX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D462=TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-punc">
                            <mjx-c clas=s=3D"mjx-c2C"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"2">
                            <mjx-c c=lass=3D"mjx-c1D463 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml un=selectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/=Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>u</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-punc">,</m= o>
                                <mi>v</mi>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9=">.
            </span><span class=3D" cmr-9">We propose a spectral algorithm: c=
                    onstruct the function </span>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"562" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D719 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </m= jx-mi>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-punc" space=3D"4">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mj=x-c3A"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"4">
                                <mjx-c class=3D="mjx-c1D449 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" s=pace=3D"4">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2192"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-msub space=3D"4="><mjx-mrow><mjx-mi class=3D" mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c211D TEX-A">
                                    </mjx-= c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        <mjx-script style=3D"vertical-align: -0.15em;">
                            <mjx-m= row size=3D"s">
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2=265"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c30">
                                        </mj= x-c>
                                </mjx-mn>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                        </mjx-script>
                        </mjx-msub>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-m= ml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/=1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>=CF=95</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-=punc">:</mo>
                            <mi>V</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=E2=86=92</mo>
                            <msub>
                                <mrow=>
                                    <mi>=E2=84=9D</mi>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=E2=89=A5</mo>
                                        <= mn>0</mn>
                                    </mrow>
                            </msub>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                </mjx-container><span cla=ss=3D"cmr-9">,</span>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"563" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D719 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </m= jx-mi>

                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx=-c3D"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-n qopname" space=3D"4">
                                <mjx-c c=lass=3D"mjx-c61"></mjx-c>
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c72"></mjx-c>
                                <mjx-c class=3D"m=jx-c67"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"2">
                                <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c2061"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-msub>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-n=qopname">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6D"></mjx-c>
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c69"></mjx-c>=
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c6E"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"=2">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2061"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mo>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-script style==3D"vertical-align: -0.313em;">
                                    <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                        <mjx-mfrac>
                                            <mjx-frac at=op=3D"true" style=3D"vertical-align: -0.345em;">
                                                <mjx-num style=3D"padding-b=ottom: 0.312em;">
                                                    <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                            <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c1D453 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-pun=c">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c3A"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                            <mjx-c= class=3D"mjx-c1D449 TEX-I">
                                                                </mjx-c>

                                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathCla=ss-rel">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2192"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mo>
                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i"=>
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c211D TEX-A"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                                </mjx-num>
                                                <mj= x-den>
                                                    <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4=53 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                        </mjx-mi>
                                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open"=>
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mo>
                                                            <mjx-mrow>
                                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i="><mjx-c class=3D" mjx-c1D462
                                                                    TEX-I">
                                                                    </mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                                            <mjx-mo cla=ss=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                                            </mjx-mo>
                                                </mj= x-mrow>
                                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c3D">
                                                        </mjx= -c>

                                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inli=ne">
                                            <mi>=CF=95</mi>
                                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                </mjx-container>
                                <span class=3D"cmr-9">is the smallest. This algorithm provably =
                                    terminates and results in a short path from
                                </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_A=ttached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"571" style=3D"f=ont-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hid=den=3D"true">
                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D463 TEX-I">
                                                </mj= x-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                    </mjx-math>
                                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"i=nline"><math
                                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline="><mi>v</mi></math></mjx-assistive-mml></mjx-container>
            <span class=3D" cmr-9">to </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                            <mjx-container cl=ass=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"
                                                tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_c=ounter=3D"572" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%;
                                                position: relative;">
                                                <mjx-math c=lass=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                        <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c1D462 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                                </mjx-math>
                                                <mjx-assistive-mml unsele=ctable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                        xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math=/MathML"
                                                        display=3D"inline">
                                                        <mi>u</mi>
                                                    </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                                </mjx-cont= ainer><span class=3D"cmr-9">,
                                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">often the shortest. The efficiency=
                                                    of this method is due to a discrete analogue
                                                    of a phenomenon in
                                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">Partial Differential Equations tha=
                                                    t is not well understood. We prove optimality
                                                    for trees and discuss
                                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">a number of open questions.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Stonyakin2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-394" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Stonyakin, Fedor et al. =E2=80=9CInexact Rel=
            ative Smoothness and Strong Convexity for Optimization
            and Variational Inequalities by Inexact Model=E2=80=9D. In: (Ja=
            n. 2020). arXiv: 2001.09013 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[math.OC]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper we propose a
                general algorithmic framework for first-order methods in
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">optimization in a broad sense, inc=
                    luding minimization problems, saddle-point
                    problems and variational
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">inequalities. This framework allow=
                    s to obtain many known methods as a special
                    case, the list including
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">accelerated gradient method, compo=
                    site optimization methods, level-set methods,
                    Bregman proximal
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">methods. The idea of the framework=
                    is based on constructing an inexact model of
                    the main problem
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">component, i.e. objective function=
                    in optimization or operator in variational
                    inequalities. Besides
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">reproducing known results, our fra=
                    mework allows to construct new methods, which
                    we illustrate by
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">constructing a universal condition=
                    al gradient method and universal method for
                    variational inequalities
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">with composite structure. These me=
                    thod works for smooth and non-smooth problems
                    with optimal
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">complexity without a priori knowle=
                    dge of the problem smoothness. As a particular
                    case of our general
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">framework, we introduce relative s=
                    moothness for operators and propose an
                    algorithm for VIs with
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">such operator. We also generalize =
                    our framework for relatively strongly convex
                    objectives and strongly
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">monotone variational inequalities.=
                    This paper is an extended and updated version
                    of [arXiv:1902.00990].
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">In particular, we add an extension=
                    of relative strong convexity for optimization
                    and variational
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">inequalities.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Su2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-395" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Su, Jiya et al. =E2=80=9CCapelliniSpTRSV: A =
            Thread-Level Synchronization-Free Sparse Triangular
            Solve on GPUs=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">Proceedings=
                of the 49th International Conference on Parallel
                Processing</span>.
            ICPP 2020. ACM, Aug. 2020. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class==3D"small-caps">doi</span></span>:
            10.1145/3404397.3404400.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Sparse triangular solves
                (SpTRSVs) have been extensively used in linear algebra field=
                s,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and many GPU-based SpTRSV algorith=
                    ms have been proposed. Synchronization-free
                    SpTRSVs, due
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">to their short preprocessing time =
                    and high performance, are currently the most
                    popular SpTRSV
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">algorithms. However, we observe th=
                    at the performance of those SpTRSV algorithms
                    on different
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">matrices can vary greatly by 845 t=
                    imes. Our further studies show that when the
                    average number
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of components per level is high an=
                    d the average number of nonzero elements per
                    row is low, those
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">SpTRSVs exhibit extremely low perf=
                    ormance. The reason is that, they use a warp on
                    the GPU to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">process a row in sparse matrices, =
                    and such warp-level designs have severe
                    underutilization of the GPU.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">To solve this problem, we propose =
                    CapelliniSpTRSV, a thread-level
                    synchronization-free SpTRSV
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">algorithm. Particularly, Capellini=
                    SpTRSV has three novel features. First, unlike
                    the previous studies,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">CapelliniSpTRSV does not need prep=
                    rocessing to calculate levels. Second,
                    CapelliniSpTRSV exhibits
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">high performance on matrices that =
                    previous SpTRSVs cannot handle efficiently.
                    Third, CapelliniSpTRSV=E2=80=99s
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">optimization does not rely on spec=
                    ific sparse matrix storage format. Instead, it
                    can achieve very good

                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">performance on the most popular sp=
                    arse matrix storage, compressed sparse row
                    (CSR) format, and thus
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">users do not need to conduct forma=
                    t conversion. We evaluate CapelliniSpTRSV with
                    245 matrices from
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the Florida Sparse Matrix Collecti=
                    on on three GPU platforms, and experiments show
                    that our SpTRSV
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">exhibits 6.84 GFLOPS/s, which is 4=
                    .97</span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0=" jax=3D" CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"573" style=3D"font-size:=117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"tr=ue">
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-cD7"></mjx-c>=
                        </mjx-mo>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inlin=e"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <m= o class=3D"MathClass-bin">=C3=97</mo>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-conta= iner>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">speedup over the state-of-the-art synchro=
                        nization-free SpTRSV algorithm, and 4.74</span>
                    <!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"574" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-cD7">
                                    </mjx-= c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inl=ine"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">=
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=C3=97</mo>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-con= tainer>
                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">speedup over the SpTRSV in cuSPARSE. Cape=
                            lliniSpTRSV is open-sourced in
                            https://github.com/JiyaSu/CapelliniSpTRSV.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Sun2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-396" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Sun, J. et al. =E2=80=9CAutomated Performanc=
            e Modeling of HPC Applications Using
            Machine Learning=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">IEEE Tra=
                nsactions on Computers </span>(2020). <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span
                    class==3D"small-caps">issn</span></span>: 2326-3814. <span class=3D"cmcsc-10">
                <sp= an class=3D"small-caps">doi
            </span></span>:
            10.1109/TC.2020.2964767.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Automated performance modeling
                and performance prediction of parallel programs are
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">highly valuable in many use cases,=
                    such as in guiding task management and job
                    scheduling, offering
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">insights of application behaviors,=
                    and assisting resource requirement estimation.
                    The performance of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">parallel programs is affected by n=
                    umerous factors, including but not limited to
                    hardware, applications,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">algorithms, and input parameters, =
                    thus an accurate performance prediction is
                    often a challenging
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">task. In this study, we focus on a=
                    utomatically predicting the execution time of
                    parallel programs
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">with different inputs, at differen=
                    t scale, and without domain knowledge. We model
                    the correlation
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">between the execution time and dom=
                    ain-independent runtime features. These
                    features include values of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">variables, counters of branches, l=
                    oops, and MPI communications. After collecting
                    data from executions
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">with different inputs, a random fo=
                    rest machine learning approach is used to build
                    an empirical
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">performance model, which can predi=
                    ct the execution time of the program given an
                    input. An instance-transfer
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">learning method is used to reuse a=
                    n existing model and improve the prediction on
                    a new platform that
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">lacks historical execution data. O=
                    ur experiments and analyses of three parallel
                    applications on three
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">different systems confirm that our=
                    method performs well, with less than </span>
                <!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"575" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                        <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c32"></mjx-c>
                            <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c30"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mn>
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c=25 TEX-MI"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"o=n" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" di=splay=3D"inline">
                            <mn>20</mn>
                            <mi>%</mi>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-cont= ainer>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">prediction error on average.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Sundar2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-397" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Sundar, Kaarthik et al. =E2=80=9CPiecewise P=
            olyhedral Formulations for a Multilinear Term=E2=80=9D. In:
            (Jan.
            2020). arXiv: 2001.00514 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[math.OC]</spa= n>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">In this paper, we present a
                mixed-integer linear programming formulation of a piecewise,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">polyhedral relaxation (PPR) of a m=
                    ultilinear term using it=E2=80=99s convex hull
                    representation. Based on the
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">solution of the PPR, we also prese=
                    nt a MIP-based piecewise formulation which
                    restricts the solutions to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">be feasible for the multilinear te=
                    rm. We then present computational results
                    showing the effectiveness of
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">proposed formulations on instances=
                    from the standard Mixed-Integer Nonlinear
                    Programming Library
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">(MINLPLib) and compare the propose=
                    d formulation with a formulation that is built
                    by recursively
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">relaxing bilinear groupings of the=
                    multilinear term, typically applied in the
                    literature.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Swenson2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-398" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Swenson, Nicolas et al. =E2=80=9CPersGNN: Ap=
            plying Topological Data Analysis and Geometric Deep
            Learning to Structure-Based Protein Function Prediction=E2=80=
            =9D. In: (Oct. 2020). arXiv: 2010.16027
            <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[q-bio.BM]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">Understanding protein
                structure-function relationships is a key challenge in compu=
                tational
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">biology, with applications across =
                    the biotechnology and pharmaceutical
                    industries. While it is known
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">that protein structure directly im=
                    pacts protein function, many functional
                    prediction tasks use only
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">protein sequence. In this work, we=
                    isolate protein structure to make functional
                    annotations for proteins
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">in the Protein Data Bank in order =
                    to study the expressiveness of different
                    structure-based prediction
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">schemes. We present PersGNN - an e=
                    nd-to-end trainable deep learning model that
                    combines graph
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">representation learning with topol=
                    ogical data analysis to capture a complex set
                    of both local and global
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">structural features. While variati=
                    ons of these techniques have been successfully
                    applied to proteins
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">before, we demonstrate that our hy=
                    bridized approach, PersGNN, outperforms either
                    method on its
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">own as well as a baseline neural n=
                    etwork that learns from the same information.
                    PersGNN achieves a
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">9.3% boost in area under the preci=
                    sion recall curve (AUPR) compared to the best
                    individual model,

                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">as well as high F1 scores across d=
                    ifferent gene ontology categories, indicating
                    the transferability of this
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">approach.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Swirydowicz2020" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-399" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">=C5=9Awirydowicz, Katarzyna et al. =E2=80=9C=
            Low synchronization Gram=E2=80=93Schmidt and generalized minimal
            residual algorithms=E2=80=9D. In: <span class=3D"cmti-10">Numer=
                ical Linear Algebra with Applications </span>(Oct.
            2020). <span class=3D"cmcsc-10"><span class=3D"small-caps">doi<= /span></span>:
                10.1002/nla.2343.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">The Gram=E2=80=93Schmidt process uses
                orthogonal projection to construct the </span><!-- l. 30 -->=
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex==3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"576" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relat=ive;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i">

                        </mjx-mi>
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx=-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c3D"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mj= x-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"4">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D444 TEX-I"></mjx-c>=
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D445 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistiv= e-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.o=rg/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                            <mi>A</mi>
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-re=l">=3D</mo>
                            <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">QR</mi>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                        </m= jx-container>
                        <span class=3D"cmr-9">factorization of a matrix. When </span>
                        <!= -- l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHT=ML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                ctxtmenu_counter=3D"577" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; pos=ition: relative;">
                                <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                    <mjx-mi=class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D444 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                    </mjx-ma= th>
                                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                            xmlns=3D="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                            <mi>Q</mi>
                                        </math></= mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-container>
                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">has linearly independent columns, the ope=
                                rator </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                            <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attache=d_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                ctxtmenu_counter=3D"578" style=3D"font-si=ze: 117.4%; position: relative;">

                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D443 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        </= mjx-mi>
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">

                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"4">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D="mjx-c1D43C TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" s=pace=3D"3">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx=-i" space=3D"3">

                                        </mjx-mi>

                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">

                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            <mjx-mrow>
                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c=1D444 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D447 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    <mjx-c class==3D"mjx-c1D444 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mi>
                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n Ma=thClass-close">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mo>
                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-mo= class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2212">
                                                    </mjx= -c>
                                                    </mjx-mo>
                                                    <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n" space=3D"3">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c31">
                                                            </= mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mn>
                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D444 TEX-I">
                                                            </= mjx-c>
                                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D447 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-math>
                                <mjx-as= sistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        xmlns=3D"http://ww=w.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>P</mi>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathCl=ass-rel">=3D</mo>
                                        <mi>I</mi>
                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                        <mi>Q<= /mi>
                                                <mrow>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                    <mrow>
                                                        <mi mathvariant=3D"itali=c">QTQ</mi>
                                                    </mrow>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                                </mrow>
                                                <mo class=3D"M=athClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>
                                                <mn>1</mn>
                                                <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">QT</mi>
                                                </m= ath>
                                                </mjx-assistive-mml>
                            </mjx-container>
                            <span class=3D"cmr-9">defines an orthogonal projection onto </s= pan><!-- l. 30 -->
                                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax==3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"579" style=3D"font-size: 117.=4%; position: relative;">
                                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                        <= mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D444 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                            <= mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-rel" space=3D"4">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c22A5"=></mjx-c>
                                                </mjx-mo>
                                    </mjx-math>
                                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display==3D"inline"><math
                                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"i=nline">
                                            <mi>Q</mi>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=E2=8A=A5</mo>
                                        </math></mjx-ass= istive-mml>
                                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">.
                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">In finite precision, </span>
                                <!-- l=
. 30 -->
                                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML"=tabindex=3D"0"
                                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"580" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; positio=n: relative;">
                                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                        <mjx-mi clas=s=3D"mjx-i">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D444 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                    </mjx-math>
                                    <= mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                            xmlns=3D"htt=p://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                            <mi>Q</mi>
                                        </math></mjx-= assistive-mml>
                                </mjx-container>
                                <span class=3D"cmr-9">loses orthogonality as the factorization =
                                    progresses. A family of approximate projections
                                    is derived with
                                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the form </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                <mjx= -container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"=0"
                                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"581" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position:

                                        </mj= x-mi>
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin" space=3D"3">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-=c2212"></mjx-c>

                                </mjx= -c>

                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">,
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">with correction matrix </span>
                <!--=

                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML=" tabindex=3D" 0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"582" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; posit=ion: relative;">

                </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">.
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">When </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-con= tainer class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    c=txtmenu_counter=3D"583" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">

                        </mjx-mo>
                        <mjx-mrow s=pace=3D"4">
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28">=
                                </mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4=44 TEX-I"></mjx-c>

                                </mjx= -c>
                        </mjx-mn>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"in=line"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline"=>
                                <mi>T</mi>
                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-rel">=3D</mo>

                                        <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">QTQ</mi>

                                    <mo class=3D"M=athClass-close">)</mo>

                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=E2=88=92</mo>

                                    1</mn>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                        </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">,
                        </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">and </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                        <mjx-cont= ainer class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                            ct=xtmenu_counter=3D"584" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                            <mj= x-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-= c class=3D"mjx-c1D447 TEX-I">
                                        </mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml= unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/19=98/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi>T</mi>
                                    </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </m= jx-container>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">is triangular, it is postulated that the =
                        best achievable orthogonality is </span>
                    <!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached=_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"585" style=3D"font-siz=e: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"=true">
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-sc mjx-b mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4AA TEX-S=C"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-=open">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"=mjx-i">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D700 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-m= o class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mo=>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D705 TEX-I">
                                    </mjx= -c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                    <m= jx-c class=3D"mjx-c28">
                                        </mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                        <= mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D434 TEX-I">
                                            </mjx-c>
                                    </mjx-mi>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mo class==3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                </mjx-= mrow>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline">
                            <= math xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mi ma=thvariant=3D"bold-script">=F0=9D=92=AA</mi>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-ope=n">(</mo>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <mi>=F0=9D=9C=80</mi>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)<= /mo>
                                </mrow>
                                <mi>=CE=BA</mi>
                                <mrow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                    <mrow>
                                        <m= i>A</mi>
                                    </mrow>
                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                </mrow></math></mjx-assi= stive-mml>
                    </mjx-container><span class=3D"cmr-9">.
                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">We present new variants of modifie=
                        d (MGS) and classical Gram=E2=80=93Schmidt algorithms
                        that require one
                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">global reduction step. An interest=
                        ing form of the projector leads to a compact
                    </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_A=ttached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"586" style=3D"f=ont-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hid=den=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D44A TEX-I">
                                    </mj= x-c>
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D44C TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mjx-assi= stive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http://www.=w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">WY</= mi>
                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                    </mjx-container>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">representation for MGS. In particular, th=
                        e inverse compact </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax Ctxt=Menu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"587" styl=e=3D"font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" ar=ia-hidden=3D"true">
                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D44A TEX-I="></mjx-c><mjx-c class=3D" mjx-c1D44C TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                        </mjx-math>
                        <mj= x-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                xmlns=3D"http:=//www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                <mi mathvariant=3D"italic=">WY</mi></math></mjx-assistive-mml></mjx-container>
            <span class=3D" cmr-9">MGS algorithm is equivalent to a lower tr=
                                    iangular solve. Our main contribution is to
                                    introduce a
                                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">backward normalization lag into th=
                                        e compact </span><!-- l. 30 -->
                                    <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Att=ached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                                        ctxtmenu_counter=3D"588" style=3D"fon=t-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                                        <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidde=n=3D"true">
                                            <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D44A TEX-I">
                                                    </mjx-= c>
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D44C TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                            </mjx-mi>
                                        </mjx-math>
                                        <mjx-assist= ive-mml unselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                                xmlns=3D"http://www.w3=.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                                <mi mathvariant=3D"italic">WY</mi=>
                                            </math></mjx-assistive-mml>
                                    </mjx-container>
                                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">representation, resulting in a </span>
                                    <!-= - l. 30 -->
                                        <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTM=L"
                                            tabindex=3D"0" ctxtmenu_counter=3D"589" style=3D"font-size: 117.4%;
                                            posi=tion: relative;">
                                            <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-hidden=3D"true">
                                                <mjx-mi c=lass=3D"mjx-sc mjx-b mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D4AA TEX-SC"></mjx-c>
                        </mj= x-mi>
                        <mjx-mrow space=3D"2">
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c28"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                            <mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                    <mjx-c c=lass=3D"mjx-c1D700 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mi>
                            </mjx-mrow>
                            <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-=n MathClass-close">
                                <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mo>
                        </mjx-mrow>
                        <mj= x-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D705 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                            </mjx-mi>
                            <mj= x-mrow space=3D"2">
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-open">
                                    <mjx-c class=3D"m=jx-c28"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                <mjx-mrow>
                                    <mjx-mrow>
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathCla=ss-open">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c5B"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        <mjx-mrow>
                                            <mjx-msub>
                                                <mjx= -mrow>
                                                    <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45F TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                        </m= jx-mi>
                                        </mjx-mrow>
                                        <mjx-script style=3D"vertical-align: -0.15em;">
                                            <mjx-mrow s=ize=3D"s">
                                                <mjx-mn class=3D"mjx-n">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c30"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mn=>
                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                        </mjx-script>
                                        </mjx-msub>
                                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-punc">=
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c2C"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mo>
                                        <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i" space=3D"=2">
                                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D434 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx-mi>
                                        <mjx-msub>
                                            <mjx-mrow>
                                                <= mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D449 TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                    </mjx-mi>
                                                    <= /mjx-mrow>
                                                        <mjx-script style=3D"vertical-align: -0.15em; margin-left:
                                                            -0.186=em;">
                                                            <mjx-mrow size=3D"s">
                                                                <mjx-mi class=3D"mjx-i">
                                                                    <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c1D45=A TEX-I"></mjx-c>
                                                                </mjx-mi>
                                                            </mjx-mrow>
                                                        </mjx-script>
                                        </mjx-msub>
                                    </mjx-mrow>
                                    <mj= x-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                        <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-c5D"></mjx-c>
                                        </mjx= -mo>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-close">
                                    <mjx-c cl=ass=3D"mjx-c29"></mjx-c>
                                </mjx-mo>
                                </mjx-mrow>
                                </mjx-math>
                                <mjx-assistive-mml u=nselectable=3D"on" display=3D"inline"><math
                                        xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998=/Math/MathML" display=3D"inline">
                                        <mi mathvariant=3D"bold-script">=F0=9D=92=
                                            =AA</mi>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                            <mrow>
                                                <mi>=F0=9D=9C=80</mi=>
                                            </mrow>
                                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                        </mrow>
                                        <mi>=CE=BA</mi>
                                        <mrow>
                                            <mo= class=3D"MathClass-open">(</mo>
                                                <mrow>
                                                    <mrow>
                                                        <mo class=3D"MathClass-open">[<= /mo>
                                                                <mrow>
                                                                    <msub>
                                                                        <mrow>
                                                                            <mi>r</mi>
                                                                        </mrow>
                                                                        <mrow>
                                                                            <mn>0</mn>
                                                                        </mrow>
                                                                    </msub>
                                                                    <mo cl=ass=3D"MathClass-punc">,</mo>
                                                                    <mi>A</mi>
                                                                    <msub>
                                                                        <mrow>
                                                                            <mi>V</mi>
                                                                        </mrow>
                                                                        <mrow>
                                                                            <= mi>m</mi>
                                                                        </mrow>
                                                                    </msub>
                                                                </mrow>
                                                                <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">]</mo>
                                                    </mrow>
                                                    </= mrow>
                                                    <mo class=3D"MathClass-close">)</mo>
                                                </mrow>
                                    </math></mjx-assistive-mml>=
                    </mjx-container>
                    <span class=3D"cmr-9">stable Generalized Minimal Residual Metho=
                        d (GMRES) algorithm that requires only one
                        global reduce
                    </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">per iteration. Further improvement=
                        s in performance are achieved by accelerating
                        GMRES on GPUs.</span>
        </p>
    </dd>
    <dt id=3D"X0-Tan2020a" class=3D"thebibliography">
    </dt>
    <dd id=3D"bib-400" class=3D"thebibliography">
        <!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent">Tan, Cheng et al. =E2=80=9CARENA: Asynchrono=
            us Reconfigurable Accelerator Ring to Enable
            Data-Centric Parallel Computing=E2=80=9D. In: (Nov. 2020). arXi=
            v: 2011.04931 <span class=3D"cmtt-10">[cs.DC]</span>.
        </p><!-- l. 30 -->
        <p class=3D"noindent"><span class=3D"cmti-9">Abstract: </span>
            <spa= n class=3D"cmr-9">The next generation HPC and data
                centers are likely to be reconfigurable and data-centric
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">due to the trend of hardware speci=
                    alization and the emergence of data-driven
                    applications. In this
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">paper, we propose ARENA =E2=80=93 =
                    an asynchronous reconfigurable accelerator ring
                    architecture as a potential
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">scenario on how the future HPC and=
                    data centers will be like. Despite using the
                    coarse-grained
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">reconfigurable arrays (CGRAs) as t=
                    he substrate platform, our key contribution is
                    not only the CGRA-cluster
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">design itself, but also the ensemb=
                    le of a new architecture and programming model
                    that enables
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">asynchronous tasking across a clus=
                    ter of reconfigurable nodes, so as to bring
                    specialized computation
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">to the data rather than the revers=
                    e. We presume distributed data storage without
                    asserting any prior
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">knowledge on the data distribution=
                    . Hardware specialization occurs at runtime
                    when a task finds
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">the majority of data it requires a=
                    re available at the present node. In other
                    words, we dynamically
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">generate specialized CGRA accelera=
                    tors where the data reside. The asynchronous
                    tasking for bringing
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">computation to data is achieved by=
                    circulating the task token, which describes
                    the data-flow graphs to
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">be executed for a task, among the =
                    CGRA cluster connected by a fast ring network.
                    Evaluations on a set
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">of HPC and data-driven application=
                    s across different domains show that ARENA can
                    provide better
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">parallel scalability with reduced =
                    data movement (53.9%). Compared with
                    contemporary compute-centric
                </span><span class=3D"cmr-9">parallel models, ARENA can bring o=
                    n average 4.37</span><!-- l. 30 -->
                <mjx-container class=3D"MathJax CtxtMenu=_Attached_0" jax=3D"CHTML" tabindex=3D"0"
                    ctxtmenu_counter=3D"590" style=3D="font-size: 117.4%; position: relative;">
                    <mjx-math class=3D"MJX-TEX" aria-h=idden=3D"true">
                        <mjx-mo class=3D"mjx-n MathClass-bin">
                            <mjx-c class=3D"mjx-cD=7"></mjx-c>
                        </mjx-mo>
                    </mjx-math>
                    <mjx-assistive-mml unselectable=3D"on" displ=ay=3D"inline"><math
                            xmlns=3D"http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display=3D="inline">
                            <mo class=3D"MathClass-bin">=C3=97</mo>
                        </math></mjx-assistive-mml>=
                </mjx-container>
                <span class=3D"cmr-9">speedup. The synthesized CGRAs and their =
                    task-dispatchers only occupy 2.93</span>
